Title: Nailed It!
Vibe: This challenge puts you alongside the build crew, where you'll help transform stacks of lumber into something that serves the compound for years to come. Every board you measure, every cut you make, and every nail you drive becomes part of the WAC's growing legacy. Long after the project is finished, you'll be able to point to it and proudly say, "I helped build that."
Why It Matters: Building teaches more than construction skills—it develops teamwork, problem-solving, patience, and pride in a job well done. Understanding how structures come together fosters confidence and appreciation for the craftsmanship behind every improvement. More importantly, it reinforces the WAC tradition that every member leaves the compound a little better than they found it.
Mission: Actively participate in the layout, measuring, cutting, and assembly of a structural building or property improvement project under the guidance of the WAC build crew.
Presidents Note: There's no such thing as a 'quick' project once someone says, 'While we're at it...

Title: Raise the Roof
Vibe: An intermediate building milestone moving you up to power tool proficiency, blueprints, and load-bearing designs.
Why It Matters: As the infrastructure demands of the compound expand, so must your technical skills. Moving beyond basic assembly means learning to read structural plans, square up corners perfectly, and manage heavy construction materials safely.
Mission: Lead or play a major role in the framing, roofing, or foundation of a WAC construction project, such as a lean-to, woodshed, deck, pavilion, or similar permanent structure.
Presidents Note: Requires a minimum of 8 verified construction hours and full mastery of framing nailers, miter saws, and leveling equipment.

Title: Heavy Lifting
Vibe: This challenge is about taking ownership of something bigger than yourself. It requires planning, leadership, persistence, and the willingness to tackle projects that leave a permanent mark on the WAC landscape. Years from now, members may not remember who won the cornhole tournament—but they'll remember who built the tower, poured the foundation, or helped bring electricity to a new corner of the property.
Why It Matters: Large infrastructure projects teach leadership, long-term planning, teamwork, and resilience. They require balancing vision with execution while solving unexpected problems along the way. More importantly, these projects reinforce one of the WAC's core principles: every generation should leave the compound stronger, more capable, and better prepared than they found it. Permanent improvements become lasting symbols of service, craftsmanship, and commitment to those who will enjoy the property for decades to come.
Mission: Actively manage or execute a major, multi-day WAC infrastructure project, such as constructing a permanent hunting blind tower, installing a solar power system, pouring structural footings, or completing another significant compound improvement approved by the WAC President.
Presidents Note: Every 'two-day project' is legally required to become a four-week project.

Title: Not in the Budget
Vibe: Long before a building becomes part of the landscape, it exists only as an idea—a sketch on paper, a conversation around the campfire, or a vision shared among family and friends. This challenge is about taking that vision and carrying it all the way to completion. From the first blueprint to the final nail, you'll guide a project that permanently changes the WAC and becomes part of its history.
Why It Matters: True leadership is measured not by the projects you complete, but by the legacy you leave for others. Managing a major construction project requires vision, organization, communication, budgeting, problem-solving, and perseverance. More importantly, it demonstrates stewardship by creating something that will continue serving the WAC long after the builders have put away their tools. Great organizations aren't built by chance—they're built by people willing to invest in a future they may never fully see.
Mission: Conceive, design, plan, secure approval and resources for, and successfully manage the complete construction of a major WAC facility or permanent compound improvement, such as a cabin addition, pavilion, workshop, storage building, bathhouse, or other landmark structure approved by the WAC President.
Presidents Note: Congratulations... you've officially learned that every blueprint is just an optimistic suggestion.

Title: Curb Appeal
Vibe: Every enhancement reflects a simple belief: the compound should never stand still. Each generation has the opportunity—and the responsibility—to leave behind something meaningful for the next.
Why It Matters: Stewardship is about more than maintaining property; it's about investing in the future. Protecting buildings from the elements extends their life for decades, while thoughtful landscaping and habitat improvements strengthen ecosystems, support wildlife, and make the WAC a more welcoming place for people and nature alike. This challenge reinforces the idea that ownership isn't measured by what you take from a place, but by what you leave behind.
Mission: Complete a major property enhancement project by either: 1. Applying exterior siding, staining, painting, sealing, or other long-term protective finishing to a primary WAC structure. OR 2. Designing and installing a permanent landscape feature or wildlife habitat area that improves the beauty, conservation value, or enjoyment of the WAC.
Presidents Note: The weeds have been holding a meeting about you... it's time to remind them who's in charge

Title: Hey Kid, Get Off My Lawn!
Vibe: Every property has its boundaries, but few people ever take the time to truly explore them. This adventure sends you on a journey around the edges of the WAC, following the land that makes this place special.
Why It Matters: The WAC isn't just where we gather—it's the foundation for countless adventures, traditions, and memories. Knowing the property lines builds an appreciation for the land, encourages exploration, and reminds us that every acre has its own character and story. You can't truly know the WAC until you've walked its boundaries.
Mission: Find and walk to all four corners of the WAC property line.
Presidents Note: Everyone thinks they know where the corners are until they're standing in the middle of the woods saying, "I'm pretty sure it's just over that hill." Spoiler alert... it's usually one more hill.

Title: Swap Till You Drop
Vibe: Every great adventure starts with a single step... or in this case, a single object. Your mission is simple: begin with something of little value and keep trading until you've completed fifteen successful exchanges. Every trade opens the door to a new conversation, a new story, and maybe even an unexpected treasure.
Why It Matters: This challenge is about far more than collecting a better item. It encourages confidence, communication, creativity, and the willingness to step outside your comfort zone. Along the way you'll meet new people, hear interesting stories, and discover that sometimes the most valuable part of a trade isn't the object you receive—it's the experience that comes with it.
Mission: Start with any object and then complete 15 trades ending with a new object at the end.
Presidents Note: Let's clear up one important rule... this isn't a garage sale and it's definitely not eBay. No buying your way to the finish line.

Title: Hillbilly Home Depot
Vibe: An old pallet becomes a campfire bench. A rusted horseshoe becomes a coat hook. Scrap steel transforms into a fire poker, lantern hanger, or one-of-a-kind piece of compound art. This challenge celebrates creativity, resourcefulness, and the satisfaction of building something useful with your own hands from materials others have given up on.
Why It Matters: Resourcefulness has always been one of the defining traits of great outdoorsmen, farmers, and craftsmen. Learning to repurpose discarded materials develops creativity, problem-solving, and practical skills while reducing waste. More importantly, every finished project tells a story. Years from now, someone may ask where that unique bench, bottle opener, or tool came from, and you'll proudly answer, "It used to be a pile of junk."
Mission: Locate a piece of discarded scrap metal, obsolete equipment, or raw pallets, and completely fabricate it into a fully functional, decorative, or utilitarian tool for the compound.
Presidents Note: Nothing at the WAC is ever officially junk.

Title: Hay There
Vibe: Every working homestead depends on people willing to build, repair, maintain, and preserve the things that keep it running. From constructing a safe home for animals to climbing onto the seat of a 1947 Farmall Cub, this adventure connects you with the traditions, hard work, and ingenuity that built rural America.
Why It Matters: Learning to build and maintain farm infrastructure develops craftsmanship, responsibility, and pride of ownership. Preserving and operating vintage equipment reminds us that older tools still have value when they're cared for properly. Together, these experiences teach stewardship—not just of the land, but of the history and traditions that make the WAC unique.
Mission: 1. Design, build, restore, or maintain a functional livestock enclosure (such as a chicken coop, rabbit hutch, or similar structure) on WAC property. 2. Safely operate the 1947 Farmall Cub under supervision (if required).
Presidents Note: Every farm project takes twice as long as you think it will.

Title: The Hide Side
Vibe: For thousands of years, hunters and craftsmen transformed raw hides into durable leather using patience, skill, and determination. Whether you're preserving an animal hide from start to finish or crafting a useful leather item with your own hands, this adventure celebrates one of humanity's oldest and most practical trades.
Why It Matters: Leatherworking is a timeless craft that blends creativity with self-reliance. Tanning a hide teaches respect for the animal by ensuring nothing goes to waste, while building leather gear develops patience, craftsmanship, and an appreciation for handmade tools. In a world filled with disposable products, creating something that can last a lifetime is a rewarding reminder that quality is earned—not bought.
Mission: Complete one of the following: 1. Flesh, scrape, salt, cure, and tan a raw animal hide into usable leather. 2. Design and craft a leather item for practical use at the WAC (knife sheath, axe mask, belt, journal cover, possibles bag, coasters, etc.).
Presidents Note: This isn't going to smell like a candle shop.

Title: Strike While it's Hot
Vibe: Long before hardware stores, every knife, hook, horseshoe, and tool began as a glowing piece of steel and a craftsman standing beside a roaring forge.
Why It Matters: Blacksmithing combines craftsmanship, patience, and problem-solving into a skill that has shaped civilization. Forging steel teaches respect for traditional trades, precision under pressure, and the satisfaction of creating something useful with your own hands. Every finished piece carries the unmistakable marks of its maker, making it both functional and deeply personal.
Mission: Heat a piece of raw iron or steel in a high-temperature forge and successfully shape, taper, draw out, and quench it into a functional tool (such as a knife blade, fire poker, or camp hook).
Presidents Note: Once you've spent a day at the forge, you'll start looking at every bent piece of steel around the WAC and thinking, "you know what that could be."

Title: Jarheads
Vibe: The harvest isn't over when the last tomato is picked or the last deer is processed—it's over when the pantry is full.
Why It Matters: Food preservation is one of the oldest and most practical self-reliance skills. Learning to can, dehydrate, smoke, cure, or vacuum-seal food reduces waste, extends the value of every harvest, and builds confidence in providing for yourself and others. Whether you're preserving vegetables from the garden or venison from the woods, these skills connect modern families with traditions that have sustained generations.
Mission: Successfully complete all three food preservation methods: 1. Water-bath or pressure-can a batch of locally grown produce. 2. Dehydrate a batch of jerky, fruit leather, or another preserved food. 3. Cure, smoke, or vacuum-seal a wild game or meat harvest for long-term storage.
Presidents Note: Nothing humbles a grown adult faster than realizing they forgot to label twelve identical mason jars.

Title: Model Behavior
Vibe: Piece by piece, history comes back to life as plastic transforms into a World War II fighter, a legendary battleship, a classic muscle car, or a detailed replica worthy of display. This challenge rewards patience, precision, and attention to detail while celebrating the craftsmanship behind one of the world's most enduring hobbies.
Why It Matters: Scale modeling develops patience, concentration, fine motor skills, and an appreciation for history and engineering. Every carefully placed decal, brushstroke, and glued component teaches that great accomplishments are built one small step at a time. The finished model becomes more than a display piece—it becomes a reminder that persistence creates remarkable results.
Mission: Successfully assemble, glue, paint, and finish a detailed scale model of a historic vehicle, aircraft, ship, military subject, structure, or other display-quality project.
Presidents Note: The floor has a 100% success rate of catching the one tiny piece you absolutely can't replace.

Title: Happy Little Trees
Vibe: The canvas is blank. The mountains don't exist. The trees are only in your imagination. Then, one brushstroke at a time, a peaceful landscape begins to emerge. This adventure isn't about becoming a professional artist—it's about slowing down, embracing creativity, and discovering that sometimes the journey is far more rewarding than the finished painting.
Why It Matters: Painting encourages patience, creativity, and confidence by reminding us that perfection isn't the goal—expression is. Creating a landscape from a blank canvas teaches observation, focus, and the courage to try something outside your comfort zone. Whether your masterpiece ends up framed in the cabin or tucked away in a closet, you'll always remember the satisfaction of creating something that didn't exist before.
Mission: Sit down in front of a blank canvas and completely execute a landscape painting featuring classic 'happy little trees' and mountains using oil or acrylic paint.
Presidents Note: If your mountain accidentally turns into a potato, congratulations... it's now an artistic interpretation.

Title: Knot on My Watch
Vibe: For centuries, sailors, outdoorsmen, climbers, hunters, and pioneers relied on a handful of dependable knots to secure loads, rescue companions, pitch shelters, and solve everyday problems. This challenge turns a simple rope into one of the most versatile tools you'll ever carry by mastering seven of the most useful knots every adventurer should know.
Why It Matters: Knot tying is a practical skill that blends knowledge with muscle memory. A properly tied knot can secure equipment, improve safety, and solve countless problems in the outdoors, while the wrong knot—or the right knot tied incorrectly—can create unnecessary risk. Learning not only how to tie each knot but also when to use it builds confidence and reinforces the value of preparation and craftsmanship.
Mission: From memory, correctly tie and explain the practical use of the following seven knots: Bowline, Clove Hitch, Square Knot, Taut-Line Hitch, Figure-Eight Loop, Trucker's Hitch, and Sheepshank.
Presidents Note: If you have to cut the rope to untie your knot... you may need a little more practice.

Title: Sit Happens
Vibe: Not every adventure needs to get your heart racing. Sometimes the greatest escape is simply finding a quiet place to sit, breathe, and enjoy the moment.
Why It Matters: Life moves fast, and too often we forget to slow down long enough to appreciate it. Creating a peaceful retreat encourages mindfulness, relaxation, and a deeper appreciation for nature. It becomes a place to drink your morning coffee, watch a sunset, read a good book, or simply sit in silence. Everyone deserves a place that helps them recharge.
Mission: Create a small garden or sitting area for complete relaxation.
Presidents Note: Here's something I've learned over the years... adventure isn't always found at full throttle. Sometimes it's found in a quiet chair, watching the wind move through the trees while absolutely nothing is on your schedule.

Title: Ghosted
Vibe: Your hands guide it, shape it, and occasionally collapse it back into... another lump of clay. But with patience, practice, and a little creativity, that spinning mound becomes something uniquely yours. Whether it's your morning coffee mug, a hearty soup bowl, or a rustic crock for the cabin, every fingerprint tells the story of the person who made it.
Why It Matters: Pottery teaches patience, creativity, and perseverance. It reminds us that useful, beautiful things don't have to come from a factory—they can come from our own hands. Every finished piece becomes part of the WAC's story, carrying memories long after the clay has hardened.
Mission: Successfully shape, fire, glaze, and finish a functional piece of stoneware pottery (such as a coffee mug, bowl, tankard, or compound crock) using a pottery wheel or traditional hand-building techniques.
Presidents Note: If your first mug holds coffee without leaking... you're already ahead of most beginners.

Title: Whittle It Be?
Vibe: A rough branch or block of wood may not look like much at first, but hidden inside is a spoon, a walking stick, a woodland animal, or a keepsake waiting to be revealed. With nothing more than a sharp blade, steady hands, and a little imagination, you'll transform an ordinary piece of wood into something both useful and uniquely your own.
Why It Matters: Woodcarving is one of humanity's oldest crafts, blending patience, creativity, and precision into a timeless skill. It teaches respect for both your tools and your materials while encouraging focus and craftsmanship. The finished piece becomes more than just carved wood—it becomes a reminder that lasting things are often created slowly, one careful cut at a time.
Mission: Using a carving knife or whittling tools, transform a block of wood or natural branch into a recognizable three-dimensional figure or functional item such as a spoon, walking stick, ornament, or campsite tool.
Presidents Note: Remember—the goal is to carve the wood... not your thumb.

Title: Blue Ribbon Adventures
Vibe: The smell of fresh popcorn, the sound of kids laughing on carnival rides, the excitement of livestock shows, live music drifting through the fairgrounds, and neighbors catching up after another year apart...
Why It Matters: Local fairs preserve traditions that helped build our communities. They celebrate agriculture, craftsmanship, volunteerism, and the hard work of local families while providing opportunities for neighbors to reconnect. Supporting these events helps keep those traditions alive and reminds us that some of the richest experiences in life happen close to home—not at expensive destinations, but in the heart of our own communities.
Mission: Physically get out and fully attend a local 4H fair, county fair, or other authorized public cultural community gathering.
Presidents Note: Sometimes it's found just down the road, surrounded by blue ribbons, dusty boots, and the best people you'll ever meet.

Title: Neighbor by Choice
Vibe: The WAC isn't just a place—it's a community. Every friendship starts with a simple introduction, a conversation, or an unexpected moment that turns a stranger into someone you're happy to see again. This adventure challenges you to step outside your comfort zone, meet someone new, and discover that the best neighbors often become lifelong friends.
Why It Matters: Strong communities are built one relationship at a time. Taking the initiative to meet someone new strengthens neighborhoods, creates support networks, and reminds us that genuine friendships rarely happen by accident. They happen because someone was willing to take the first step and say hello.
Mission: Meet, introduce yourself to, and establish a genuine friendship or lasting acquaintance with someone new from the local WAC community.
Presidents Note: Every one of your closest friends was once someone you had never met.

Title: Respect for the Fallen
Vibe: Long before the Workman Adventure Compound existed, men and women answered a call greater than themselves. Some never returned home. Others carried the weight of their service until the end of their lives. Memorial Day is more than the beginning of summer—it's a day to pause, remember, and ensure that the stories of those who served are never forgotten.
Why It Matters: The names engraved on monuments, headstones, and memorials represent real people who laughed, dreamed, loved their families, and willingly served something greater than themselves. By sharing their stories and participating in acts of remembrance, we preserve their legacy for future generations. Remembrance isn't passive—it is a responsibility passed from one generation to the next.
Mission: Honor the memory of America's fallen by completing all of the following: Gather WAC members and share the story of a loved one, friend, or service member whose sacrifice or service deserves to be remembered. Complete at least one act of remembrance: clean veterans' graves, participate in a flag-folding ceremony, learn and perform Taps, or create a lasting tribute to a fallen hero. Observe the National Moment of Remembrance by pausing all WAC activities at 3:00 PM on Memorial Day for one minute of silence.
Presidents Note: Bring your passion, get fully involved, and present your volunteer work and its community impact to the board for final tracking.

Title: Leave it Better
Vibe: Whether you're cleaning a park, coaching a youth team, helping a local charity, supporting first responders, or lending a hand to someone in need, this challenge is about making a positive difference without expecting anything in return. It's a reminder that the true measure of an adventurer isn't just where they've been—it's the lives they've touched along the way.
Why It Matters: Communities don't become great by accident. They become great because ordinary people choose to give their time, talents, and energy for the benefit of others. Volunteering builds stronger neighborhoods, inspires future generations, and demonstrates that service isn't something we do because we have to—it's something we do because we care. Every hour invested is an investment in the place we proudly call home.
Mission: Dedicate your personal time, without compensation, to complete a meaningful community service project benefiting the WAC community or surrounding area.
Presidents Note: Leave every trail, every campsite, every community... and every person... a little better than you found them.

Title: No One Left Behind
Vibe: Across every town are veterans who quietly continue their lives after serving something greater than themselves. Some carry visible wounds. Others carry burdens no one can see. This adventure isn't about charity—it's about respect. It's about taking the time to recognize a veteran, thank them for their service, and remind them that their sacrifices have not been forgotten.
Why It Matters: Military service creates a lifelong bond built on sacrifice, commitment, and selfless service. While many veterans transition successfully, others face challenges that can only be eased through community, friendship, and genuine human connection. A simple act of kindness, practical assistance, or sincere appreciation can have a lasting impact. Supporting veterans isn't reserved for holidays—it's an opportunity that exists every day.
Mission: Identify a local military veteran and complete a meaningful act of service, support, or appreciation that makes a positive difference in their life.
Presidents Note: Do it because someone, somewhere, once raised their right hand and volunteered to stand in harm's way for people they would likely never meet.

Title: The Land Before Us
Vibe: Long before roads, towns, and cabins dotted the landscape, the forests, rivers, and lakes surrounding the WAC were home to thriving Native communities whose traditions continue today. This adventure invites you to step beyond the pages of history and experience that heritage firsthand—through celebration, storytelling, art, music, and places that continue to preserve and strengthen Native culture.
Why It Matters: Understanding the people who first called this land home deepens our appreciation for the places we explore today. Powwows, museums, and tribal cultural centers preserve traditions that have been passed from generation to generation, while tribally owned enterprises demonstrate the resilience and continued success of Native communities. Learning about another culture broadens our perspective and reminds us that every landscape has stories that began long before we arrived.
Mission: Expand your understanding of Native American heritage by completing both of the following: Attend a Native American powwow, cultural festival, museum, or tribal history center. Spend one full day visiting a tribally owned destination, such as Soaring Eagle Casino or Turtle Creek Casino.
Presidents Note: It's about discovering the stories that were already there waiting for us.

Title: The Keeper of the Keys
Vibe: Someone has to unlock the gate, inspect the property, make sure everything is safe, and prepare the WAC for another season of memories. This challenge transforms you from a visitor into a caretaker by teaching you how the compound operates, who serves the community, and what responsibilities come with protecting a place that means so much to so many.
Why It Matters: Stewardship begins with knowledge. Understanding local ordinances, emergency services, township resources, and WAC operating procedures ensures the compound remains safe, welcoming, and ready for everyone to enjoy. Every member who completes this adventure becomes another trusted caretaker capable of helping preserve the WAC for future generations.
Mission: Demonstrate proficiency in WAC operations by completing all of the following: Demonstrate a working knowledge of local ordinances and WAC policies. Locate the township hall and identify its operating hours. Identify the agencies responsible for law enforcement, fire protection, EMS, and local waste disposal services. Successfully complete the official WAC Cabin Opening Checklist.
Presidents Note: The first person to arrive at the WAC each year carries a special responsibility. They're not just opening a cabin—they're preparing an entire adventure compound for family and friends.

Title: A letter Worth Writting
Vibe: A handwritten letter requires something far more valuable than money—it requires time, thought, and sincerity. In a world of quick texts and forgotten emails, putting pen to paper reminds someone that their service, sacrifice, and commitment truly mattered. Sometimes the simplest words become the ones remembered forever.
Why It Matters: Service often happens quietly. Military members and first responders spend countless hours protecting others without expecting recognition. Taking the time to express genuine gratitude strengthens the connection between those who serve and the communities they protect. More importantly, it reminds us that appreciation should be expressed while people are still here to hear it.
Mission: Write and deliver a thoughtful, handwritten letter to a veteran, active-duty service member, or first responder expressing your appreciation, sharing how their service has impacted you, or explaining why you consider them a hero.
Presidents Note: Some heroes carry medals. Others carry memories. Every one of them deserves to know they made a difference.

Title: Embrace the Suck
Vibe: Every scrape, muddy shoe, and aching muscle becomes proof that you kept moving when stopping would've been easier. And when you finally cross the finish line covered head to toe in dirt, you'll realize the mud wasn't something to avoid.
Why It Matters: Obstacle course racing combines endurance, strength, agility, problem-solving, and mental toughness in ways that traditional races cannot. Every obstacle demands adaptability and perseverance, while every mile reinforces resilience and determination. More importantly, these events remind us that meaningful accomplishments often require embracing discomfort, helping others over obstacles, and finishing what we started—even when it gets messy.
Mission: Successfully register for, participate in, and cross the official finish line of a verified mud run or obstacle course race, such as a Spartan Race, Tough Mudder, Savage Race, Warrior Dash, or other WAC Board-approved event.
Presidents Note: Somewhere on this course, you'll question your life choices.

Title: Branch Manager
Vibe: One obstacle at a time, you'll climb higher into the forest canopy, balancing across suspended bridges, crawling through rope tunnels, leaping between platforms, and trusting your harness a little more with every step. What looked simple from the ground suddenly feels like an entirely different world once you're surrounded by treetops instead of tree trunks.
Why It Matters: Adventure often begins where comfort ends. Aerial ropes courses develop balance, coordination, problem-solving, and confidence while encouraging participants to manage fear in a safe, controlled environment. Every obstacle reinforces the value of trust—in your equipment, your training, and yourself. By the time your feet return to the ground, you'll discover that the greatest obstacle was rarely the rope bridge... it was believing you could cross it.
Mission: Successfully complete an advanced, multi-stage aerial ropes and zipline adventure course (such as TreeRunner Aerial Adventure Park or another WAC Board-approved equivalent), including all advanced elements for which the participant is eligible.
Presidents Note: The course isn't designed to test your athletic ability nearly as much as your willingness to take that next step.

Title: Saddle Sore
Vibe: Small towns become rest stops. Country roads become conversations. Hills become challenges. Camps become communities. Day after day, the miles accumulate until what once seemed impossible becomes routine. Somewhere along the journey, the ride stops being about distance and starts becoming about the people riding beside you.
Why It Matters: Long-distance cycling develops endurance, discipline, and perseverance while encouraging participants to slow down and experience the landscape in a way few other forms of travel allow. Multi-day tours reward consistency over speed, reminding riders that meaningful accomplishments are built mile by mile rather than all at once. Completing The Michigander demonstrates physical commitment, mental resilience, and a genuine appreciation for Michigan's communities, countryside, and cycling culture.
Mission: Register for and successfully complete the official multi-day Michigander Bicycle Tour, riding every required daily stage and finishing the event in accordance with official tour rules.
Presidents Note: Ummm - No E-Bikes

Title: That’s a Wrap
Vibe: Behind every great film is someone willing to wake up early for the perfect sunrise shot, climb a hill for the right camera angle, record one more interview, or spend hours editing tiny details that nobody consciously notices—but everyone appreciates. The camera becomes more than a piece of equipment; it becomes a way of preserving moments that would otherwise fade with time.
Why It Matters: Stories preserve legacies. Learning to plan, film, edit, and produce a complete video develops creativity, communication, organization, and technical skills while teaching the importance of documenting meaningful experiences. A well-crafted film allows adventures to be shared long after they've ended, ensuring that memories, traditions, and the spirit of the WAC continue to inspire future members.
Mission: Serve as the primary producer, director, editor, and storyteller to create a polished high-definition promotional film or short documentary showcasing the WAC, one of its adventures, or a significant compound event.
Presidents Note: Every masterpiece contains at least one clip that was almost deleted.

Title: Encore
Vibe: Every concert has its own soundtrack of memories—road trips with friends, songs that take you back years later, and that one performance everyone still talks about long after the speakers go silent.
Why It Matters: Live music brings people together through shared experiences that transcend age, background, and musical taste. Attending concerts encourages exploration of new artists, supports musicians and local venues, and creates lasting memories with family and friends. The WAC believes adventure isn't limited to the outdoors—sometimes it's found beneath stage lights, surrounded by thousands of voices singing the same song.
Mission: Physically attend at least three live musical performances, including concerts, amphitheater tours, music festivals, or other WAC Board-approved live music events.
Presidents Note: If your phone spent more time recording than your eyes spent watching, you missed the point.

Title: Call of the Wild
Vibe: The best zoological parks do more than display animals—they inspire curiosity, teach respect for wildlife, and remind us that many of Earth's most remarkable species depend on people willing to protect them.
Why It Matters: Modern accredited zoological parks and wildlife conservation centers play an essential role in education, scientific research, habitat restoration, and the recovery of endangered species. Visiting these facilities encourages appreciation for biodiversity while supporting organizations dedicated to protecting wildlife for future generations. Every encounter reinforces the WAC belief that adventure includes learning about—and becoming a better steward of—the natural world.
Mission: Visit and explore a premier accredited zoological park, aquarium, or wildlife conservation center recognized for excellence in animal care, conservation, education, or species recovery.
Presidents Note: Every zoo has one animal that refuses to cooperate the entire time you're there

Title: The Big Bang Theory
Vibe: A high-precision tannerite target shooting challenge testing your long-range rifle fundamentals and trigger control under pressure.
Why It Matters: Binary explosive targets detonate only when struck by a high-velocity rifle bullet traveling over 2,200 feet per second. Hitting a small target at distance requires perfect breathing control, alignment, and a steady squeeze—rewarded by a massive, satisfying boom.
Mission: Successfully detonate an authorized binary explosive target at a minimum distance of 100 yards using a centerfire rifle.
Presidents Note: Accuracy beats excuses every time.

Title: Recalculating
Vibe: There are no arrows pointing toward the finish line and no voice calmly telling you to "turn left in 500 feet." Every decision is yours alone. One bearing leads toward the next checkpoint. Another sends you wandering through hills, swamps, and thick timber wondering where things went sideways.
Why It Matters: Land navigation is one of the oldest and most valuable outdoor skills. Orienteering strengthens critical thinking, observation, decision-making, and self-reliance while teaching participants to interpret terrain rather than simply follow technology. Completing a formal navigation course builds confidence in your ability to solve problems independently and reinforces a core WAC principle: knowing where you are is the first step toward getting where you want to go.
Mission: Successfully enter and complete an organized wilderness orienteering competition or multi-point land navigation course using a map and compass on public lands or other WAC Board-approved terrain.
Presidents Note: Sometimes the shortest path isn't the best one.

Title: The Gauntlet
Vibe: At the WAC, proving yourself isn't about lifting the most weight or running the fastest mile. It's about embracing a little discomfort, laughing through the burn, and finishing what you started. The route to the deer blind, the ramp sprints, and the final celebration all tell the same story: this place was built to create memories, not perfection.
Why It Matters: The WAC Gauntlet encourages functional fitness, perseverance, and good humor. It combines cardiovascular endurance, core strength, bodyweight exercises, and a familiar course around the compound into a challenge that can be repeated year after year. More importantly, it creates a shared tradition—one that welcomes encouragement, friendly competition, and the satisfaction of completing a uniquely WAC rite of passage.
Mission: Complete the official compound fitness gauntlet within a single testing window: Run to Pauls deer blind and back, 10 pushups, 30 second planck, 3 ramp runs, 15 situps, 25 jumping jacks, two alcohol shots.
Presidents Note: The hardest part isn't the workout... it's pretending the shots are for hydration.

Title: Wet Around the Ears
Vibe: Adventure doesn't always require a long road trip. Sometimes it's waiting just down the road. Each of the lakes surrounding the WAC has its own personality, scenery, and surprises.
Why It Matters: The WAC isn't just defined by its property lines—it's part of a larger landscape filled with lakes, forests, and natural beauty. Exploring Crooked Lake, Eight Point Lake, and Big Mud Lake by kayak encourages a deeper appreciation for the area we call home while building confidence on the water. Sometimes the greatest adventures aren't found by traveling farther... they're found by looking closer.
Mission: Kayak in all three lakes near the WAC: Crooked Lake, Eight Point Lake, and Big Mud Lake.
Presidents Note: Here's the funny thing about kayaking... it looks incredibly relaxing... right up until you realize the wind has been quietly pushing you in the wrong direction for the last half hour.

Title: Splash Zone
Vibe: Leave your worries in the parking lot and prepare to spend the day chasing waves, racing down slides, floating lazy rivers, and laughing until your stomach hurts. A water park isn't about relaxing by the pool—it's about embracing your inner kid, staying active from opening until closing, and making memories one splash at a time.
Why It Matters: Growing older doesn't mean growing up. Taking an entire day to unplug, laugh, and play reminds us that adventure doesn't always have to be rugged or remote. Water parks encourage physical activity, trying new experiences, and sharing unforgettable moments with family and friends. Sometimes the healthiest thing you can do is simply spend a day having fun.
Mission: Spend a full, highly active day operating inside a major commercial water park.
Presidents Note: If you spend more time sitting in a lounge chair than going down water slides, you're doing it wrong.

Title: Putt Up or Shut Up
Vibe: One minute you're laughing at a windmill obstacle. The next you're studying bank angles like you're playing for the Masters. Every lucky bounce becomes skill. Every bad bounce becomes "a terrible course design." Somewhere around Hole 14, friendly conversation usually gives way to scorekeeping and subtle trash talk.
Why It Matters: The WAC believes adventure doesn't always require mountains, wilderness, or physical endurance. Sometimes it's about friendly competition, laughter, and spending quality time together. Miniature golf rewards patience, focus, creativity, and good sportsmanship while creating lasting memories that often become family stories. The real objective isn't just finishing under par—it's enjoying the people you're playing with.
Mission: Complete a full 18-hole round of miniature golf at three different courses. During one of those rounds, compete head-to-head against Holly and finish with a lower score.
Presidents Note: If Holly starts talking after Hole 3... you'd better start making putts.

Title: Zero Regerts
Vibe: A tattoo is one of the few adventures you carry with you every day. Every line, every shade, and every hour spent in the artist's chair becomes part of a permanent reminder of a moment, a person, a place, or a belief that mattered enough to last a lifetime.
Why It Matters: A tattoo is an expression of identity, remembrance, or personal achievement. Choosing a design thoughtfully and trusting a skilled artist to create a lasting piece encourages creativity, intentionality, and appreciation for a unique art form. Whether honoring family, military service, friendship, adventure, or personal milestones, meaningful tattoos become lifelong reminders of the experiences that shape who we are.
Mission: Undergo a complete, professional tattoo session administered by a licensed ink artist, resulting in a fully finished custom piece.
Presidents Note: The tattoo usually hurts less than explaining it to your mother.

Title: Current Situation
Vibe: The shoreline slowly fades behind you. The water stretches in every direction, and suddenly you're committed. Every paddle stroke carries you farther into the lake, where the only sounds are the wind, the water, and the rhythm you've settled into.
Why It Matters: Adventure isn't always about speed or distance. Sometimes it's about committing to a goal and seeing it through one stroke at a time. Crossing Eight Point Lake builds endurance, confidence, and an appreciation for the power and beauty of Michigan's waterways. It also reminds us that meaningful accomplishments are often achieved through steady progress rather than dramatic moments.
Mission: Complete a continuous rowing or paddling transit across the open waters of Eight Point Lake without stopping or receiving assistance.
Presidents Note: It suddenly looks a whole lot bigger than it did from shore.

Title: Junkyard Jackpot
Vibe: Every garage sale is different. One is filled with baby clothes and Christmas decorations. The next has vintage fishing lures, cast-iron cookware, an old Farmall sign, or the exact tool you've needed for years. Half the fun is the mystery. The other half is convincing yourself that you absolutely needed the rusty lantern or antique tackle box you didn't know existed five minutes earlier.
Why It Matters: Garage sales and estate sales celebrate curiosity, resourcefulness, and the appreciation of history hidden in everyday objects. They encourage conversation, negotiation, and discovering stories that might otherwise be forgotten. More importantly, they remind us that adventure doesn't always require distant travel—sometimes it's found in the next neighborhood, waiting on a folding table with a handwritten price tag.
Mission: Plan and execute a rapid garage sale, estate sale, or community rummage expedition, visiting at least five separate sales during a single weekend.
Presidents Note: The best treasure hunters know that every driveway has potential.

Title: Bubble Trouble
Vibe: The noise disappears. Breathing slows to the steady rhythm of each inhale through a regulator. Sunlight dances through the water while colorful fish, coral, sea turtles, rays, and countless other creatures continue their lives completely unaware that you've entered their world.
Why It Matters: Marine environments are among Earth's most diverse and fragile ecosystems. Experiencing them firsthand builds appreciation for ocean conservation while developing confidence, situational awareness, and respect for safety procedures. Whether hovering over a coral reef or exploring beneath the surface with scuba equipment, this adventure reminds us that some of the planet's greatest wonders exist in places most people never see.
Mission: Successfully complete a marine adventure that includes both an open-water snorkeling excursion and a certified open-water scuba dive under the supervision of a qualified dive professional.
Presidents Note: For a little while, gravity matters less, silence becomes comforting, and the underwater world reminds you just how much of our planet remains unexplored.

Title: Smooth Moves
Vibe: Roller skates bring back memories of flashing lights, music, and family skate nights. Inline skates reward speed, smooth pavement, and long summer evenings. Ice skates trade sunshine for crisp winter air, fresh ice, and the unmistakable sound of steel gliding across a frozen rink.
Why It Matters: Learning new skills keeps us adaptable, humble, and willing to laugh at ourselves. Skating improves balance, coordination, endurance, and confidence while encouraging outdoor recreation and active lifestyles throughout every season. Experiencing all three forms of skating reinforces a core WAC belief that adventure often begins by trying something just outside your comfort zone.
Mission: Successfully complete separate recreational outings using traditional quad roller skates, inline skates, and ice skates at approved public or private venues.
Presidents Note: Everyone thinks they still know how to skate... until they actually put the skates on.

Title: Frozen Assets
Vibe: Fresh snow muffles every sound. The woods become quiet. Trails that were familiar in summer transform into entirely new adventures beneath a blanket of white. Whether you're gliding across untouched snow on skis, hiking through the forest with snowshoes, or racing downhill on a sled while laughing like a kid again, winter has a way of rewarding those willing to step outside.
Why It Matters: Winter challenges us to stay active when it's easiest to stay indoors. Cold-weather recreation builds endurance, resilience, confidence, and an appreciation for one of Michigan's most beautiful seasons. It also reinforces an important WAC principle: every season offers opportunities for adventure if you're willing to dress for it and embrace the conditions instead of avoiding them.
Mission: Spend a minimum of four continuous hours participating in one or more active winter outdoor sports, such as snowshoeing, cross-country skiing, advanced sledding, or another WAC Board-approved winter activity.
Presidents Note: Winter isn't trying to stop you. It's trying to see if you mean it.

Title: Wake Up
Vibe: Whether you pop right up or spend the afternoon laughing through spectacular wipeouts, every pull teaches a little more balance, confidence, and persistence. Eventually everything clicks, and for a few unforgettable moments, you're gliding effortlessly across the lake.
Why It Matters: Tow sports develop balance, coordination, determination, and confidence while teaching participants to work through repeated failure in pursuit of success. Few outdoor activities provide a greater sense of accomplishment than standing up on the water for the first time. More importantly, they create lasting memories shared between boat crews, spotters, family, and friends—another reminder that the best adventures are often powered by teamwork.
Mission: Successfully attempt water skiing, wakeboarding, or kneeboarding behind a powered tow vessel, making a genuine effort to rise from the water and complete a controlled run.
Presidents Note: Everyone drinks a little lake water. Some just do it with more enthusiasm.

Title: Bother Bear
Vibe: Across continents and throughout history, animals have symbolized courage, wisdom, resilience, loyalty, patience, adaptability, and countless other qualities admired by the cultures that observed them. Every creature survives in its own way, relying on instincts and strengths uniquely suited to its environment.
Why It Matters: Researching animal symbolism encourages curiosity about history, anthropology, and the relationship between people and the natural world. Presenting your findings builds communication skills while challenging you to think critically about your own character. The exercise isn't about finding the "right" animal—it's about understanding why certain traits resonate with you and how they influence the way you live, lead, and explore.
Mission: Research the historical significance of animal totems across multiple cultures, design an educational presentation, and identify and defend your personal WAC animal counterpart based on its characteristics and your own personality.
Presidents Note: Choose the animal you are... not the one you wish you were.

Title: Order in the Court
Vibe: There are no dramatic background soundtracks. No perfectly timed confessions. Instead, justice moves through careful procedure, thoughtful arguments, witness testimony, and the quiet professionalism of judges, attorneys, clerks, deputies, and jurors—all working within a system designed to protect both individual rights and the rule of law.
Why It Matters: An informed citizen should understand how the judicial system functions beyond headlines and television dramas. Observing a live court proceeding provides firsthand insight into due process, constitutional protections, courtroom decorum, and the responsibilities of the people who administer justice. This experience encourages civic engagement while fostering a deeper appreciation for the institutions that uphold the rule of law.
Mission: Physically attend and observe a live trial, evidentiary hearing, or other formal judicial proceeding inside a local, state, or federal courthouse.
Presidents Note: Courtrooms don't look much like television.

Title: Code Brown
Vibe: Sooner or later, every camper, backpacker, hunter, and outdoorsman discovers that the wilderness occasionally asks very personal questions. There's no restroom sign, no running water, and no flush handle—just preparation, good judgment, and a healthy sense of humor.
Why It Matters: Backcountry sanitation is an essential outdoor skill that protects water sources, wildlife, and the experience of future visitors. Knowing how to properly manage human waste using Leave No Trace principles demonstrates responsibility, environmental stewardship, and preparedness. While the situation may be humorous, the skill is an important part of safe and ethical outdoor recreation.
Mission: Successfully complete a full emergency bowel evacuation deep in the wilderness, completely independent of modern indoor plumbing or chemical facilities.
Presidents Note: Choose your location wisely. Regret travels surprisingly far.

Title: Captains Log
Vibe: There are few feelings more peaceful than pushing away from the dock and leaving the shoreline behind.
Why It Matters: Boating builds confidence, responsibility, and an appreciation for Michigan's incredible waterways. Learning to safely operate a boat encourages good decision-making, navigation skills, and respect for the water while opening the door to countless outdoor adventures. Every experienced boater started with their very first launch.
Mission: Operate the boat and traverse a lake near the WAC
Presidents Note: Here's a little secret... boats have an amazing ability to make perfectly reasonable people forget how trailers work.

Title: Fred Bear Archery Challenge
Vibe: Whether you’re pursuing game with a bow or learning the discipline of archery on the range, this challenge honors one of mankind’s oldest hunting traditions. Success isn’t measured by speed or power—it’s earned through steady hands, ethical decisions, and countless hours of practice.
Why It Matters: Archery teaches focus, patience, discipline, and respect for both the equipment and the outdoors. Hunters develop the responsibility required to make ethical harvests, while non-hunters gain an appreciation for the precision and skill that bowhunting demands. Regardless of the path chosen, every participant leaves with a deeper understanding of one of the oldest outdoor traditions in history.
Mission: Hunters: Harvest two wild game animals using two different bow platforms (recurve, compound, or crossbow). One harvest must be a legally taken Michigan deer. Non-Hunters: Demonstrate safe operation and target proficiency with all three bow platforms under the supervision of an experienced WAC member. Score a minimum of 70/100 on a ten-arrow qualification using the bow of your choice.
Presidents Note: Every arrow tells the truth.

Title: Carnivore's Quest
Vibe: From forests and fields to rivers and ranches, every meal tells a story. This challenge invites you to broaden your palate by experiencing the incredible variety of meats that have sustained hunters, anglers, and families for generations. Some flavors will become new favorites. Others… you’ll be glad you tried once.
Why It Matters: Trying unfamiliar foods expands more than your taste buds—it deepens your appreciation for different cultures, outdoor traditions, and sustainable harvests. Every species has its own flavor, preparation, and story. Whether it comes from the woods, the water, or the pasture, each bite connects us to the people and traditions that brought it to the table.
Mission: Sample at least seven of the following thirteen meats: Whitetail Deer, Duck or Goose, Pheasant, Bison, Squirrel, Wild Turkey, Wild Hog (including bacon), Elk, Rabbit, Salmon, Trout, Dove, and Beef.
Presidents Note: The best adventures don’t just feed your stomach—they expand your horizons.

Title: The Bird Bag
Vibe: Every game bird presents a unique challenge. Success requires different habitats, different tactics, different dogs, different calls, and different seasons. This badge celebrates the complete Michigan bird hunter by encouraging versatility across the state’s remarkable variety of game birds.
Why It Matters: Michigan offers one of the most diverse game bird hunting opportunities in North America. Pursuing multiple species teaches hunters to adapt to changing habitats, refine different hunting techniques, and appreciate the conservation efforts that sustain healthy bird populations. Every successful hunt reflects patience, preparation, ethical decision-making, and respect for the resource.
Mission: Successfully identify, legally hunt, and harvest five different species of Michigan game birds. Each species must be harvested in accordance with all applicable laws and seasons.
Presidents Note: Bird hunting has a funny way of keeping your ego in check.

Title: Six on the Stringer
Vibe: Michigan’s waters offer an incredible variety of fish, each demanding different tactics, different locations, and a little patience. This challenge celebrates the journey of becoming a well-rounded angler, one species at a time.
Why It Matters: Fishing isn’t simply about catching fish—it’s about learning ecosystems, seasonal patterns, conservation, and the patience that comes with spending time on the water. Pursuing multiple species encourages anglers to explore new lakes, rivers, and techniques while gaining a greater appreciation for Michigan’s world-class fisheries.
Mission: Catch, identify, document with a photograph, and successfully land six different species of fish in Michigan waters. All fish must be legally caught in accordance with Michigan fishing regulations.
Presidents Note: The fish don’t care how expensive your gear was.

Title: Just Give it a “Fly”
Vibe: Instead of throwing a lure, you’re presenting a tiny imitation with rhythm, timing, and precision. Every cast becomes a conversation between the rod, the line, and the water. When it all comes together, it’s one of the most rewarding experiences in angling.
Why It Matters: Fly fishing teaches patience, coordination, observation, and respect for aquatic ecosystems. It encourages anglers to understand insect hatches, water currents, and fish behavior while developing casting skills that require practice rather than power. Landing your first fish on a fly you presented yourself is a milestone that many anglers never forget.
Mission: Assemble and rig a fly fishing outfit, demonstrate a competent overhead cast, and successfully catch at least one legal fish on a self-cast artificial fly.
Presidents Note: At first, you’ll wonder why it keeps wrapping around itself, landing in trees, or somehow finding the only bush within fifty feet.

Title: From Forest to Frying Pan
Vibe: Sometimes the greatest lessons come from the smallest game. Squirrels and rabbits challenge hunters to move quietly, shoot accurately, and understand the rhythms of the woods. This adventure celebrates one of Michigan's oldest hunting traditions, where patience, woodsmanship, and ethical harvesting matter far more than antler size.
Why It Matters: Small game hunting is where many outdoorsmen and women develop the skills that last a lifetime. It sharpens marksmanship, teaches safe firearm handling, improves awareness of wildlife habitat, and reinforces the importance of ethical, sustainable harvesting. More importantly, it reminds us that hunting has always been about providing food, appreciating nature, and respecting the resource—not simply chasing trophies.
Mission: Successfully harvest five small game animals (squirrels and/or rabbits) during a single legal Michigan small game season.
Presidents Note: Don't underestimate the little guys.

Title: Surf $& Turf
Vibe: One day may begin in a deer blind, continue on a quiet lake chasing fish, and finish with a flashlight scanning the shoreline for frogs. This adventure celebrates the versatility of Michigan's outdoors by challenging you to harvest from both the land and the water in a single weekend.
Why It Matters: True outdoor skills extend far beyond mastering a single season or species. Successfully harvesting game and fish within the same weekend requires planning, adaptability, and an understanding of Michigan's diverse natural resources. Adding frog hunting introduces many members to a traditional outdoor activity that connects them with wetlands, conservation, and another unique part of the state's hunting heritage.
Mission: 1. Legally harvest at least one Michigan game animal (big or small game) and catch at least one legal game fish within the same continuous 72-hour period. 2. Participate in a legal frog hunt during Michigan's open season.
Presidents Note: The greatest outdoorsmen don't specialize in one pursuit—they answer the call of every season.

Title: Trigger Time
Vibe: Every steady breath, careful trigger press, and disciplined follow-through contributes to one clean shot. This challenge celebrates the timeless skill of rifle marksmanship by emphasizing precision over power. Whether sighting in for deer season or simply improving at the range, consistent accuracy is the hallmark of a responsible rifleman.
Why It Matters: Ethical hunting begins long before opening day. Consistent practice builds confidence, reinforces firearm safety, and ensures that every shot taken in the field is deliberate and responsible. Learning the characteristics of multiple rifle calibers also develops versatility and a deeper understanding of recoil, ballistics, and proper firearm handling.
Mission: 1. Fire five consecutive shots into a 3-inch circle at 100 yards using a centerfire rifle with either iron sights or a hunting optic. 2. Safely shoot three different rifle calibers within the same 48-hour period.
Presidents Note: Anyone can brag about how far they can shoot. A true rifleman proves how well they can shoot.

Title: Breaking Clays
Vibe: There are no second chances, no stationary targets, and no shortcuts. One smooth mount, one controlled swing, and one well-timed shot determine the outcome. This challenge introduces shooters to the unique rhythm of shotgun sports while building confidence with different firearms and shooting disciplines.
Why It Matters: Shotgun shooting develops hand-eye coordination, target tracking, firearm safety, and confidence. Learning different shotgun platforms and participating in clay target sports prepares hunters for ethical shots in the field while reinforcing the safe handling habits that every responsible firearm owner should possess. More importantly, it's simply one of the most enjoyable ways to spend a day on the range with friends and family.
Mission: 1. Safely demonstrate proficiency with three different shotgun actions or gauges (e.g., pump, semi-automatic, over/under, side-by-side, single-shot; 12, 20, .410, etc.). 2. Successfully complete a round of skeet, trap, or sporting clays.
Presidents Note: You can't aim at where the target was.

Title: Tweet Dreams
Vibe: From tiny hummingbirds to majestic bald eagles, Michigan is home to an incredible variety of birds just waiting to be discovered. This year-long challenge encourages you to slow down, keep your eyes and ears open, and appreciate the beauty of nature one species at a time. Every new sighting is another reminder that the outdoors always has something new to offer.
Why It Matters: Birdwatching develops patience, observation, and a deeper appreciation for Michigan's diverse ecosystems. Once you begin identifying birds, you'll never look—or listen—to the outdoors the same way again. This challenge transforms ordinary walks, hunting trips, camping weekends, and even your own backyard into opportunities for discovery while creating a lasting connection to the natural world.
Mission: 1. Pick and Submit a start date. 2. Identify 50 different Michigan birds within one year.
Presidents Note: Here's your first lesson... every little brown bird is not a sparrow.

Title: Life After the Alarm Clock
Vibe: Adventure doesn't end when you retire—it finally has room to begin.
Why It Matters: Retirement isn't simply leaving a career—it's transitioning into a life you've intentionally prepared to enjoy. Financial security, meaningful relationships, physical and emotional well-being, and a shared vision with your spouse create the foundation for a rewarding future. The goal isn't merely to stop working. It's to gain the freedom to spend your time on the people, places, and adventures that matter most.
Mission: 1. Have good friends so you don't retire alone. 2. Hit your retirement age at the lowest age possible. 3. Be structurally debt-free with big-ticket items paid off. 4. Stop financially supporting your kids or parents. 5. Build a budget and stick to it. 6. Manage your investments across multiple income sources to avoid market crash risks. 7. Ensure your spouse is fully on board to work on your relationship as a couple and enjoy the reward.
Presidents Note: The goal isn't to retire from something. It's to retire to something.

Title: Cartoon Time Machine
Vibe: Forget streaming, smartphones, and social media. Pull on your pajamas, pour an outrageously sugary bowl of cereal, and settle into the couch for four uninterrupted hours of classic cartoons. It's a chance to relive a simpler time when Saturday mornings were sacred, cereal was practically candy, and your biggest decision was figuring out which channel had the best lineup.
Why It Matters: Some of our greatest memories aren't tied to major events—they're tied to simple traditions. Saturday morning cartoons were a weekly ritual that brought families and friends together while fueling imaginations and creating lifelong nostalgia. This challenge reminds us to occasionally slow down, unplug from today's distractions, and reconnect with the experiences that made childhood so much fun.
Mission: Travel back in time on a Saturday morning from 8:00 AM to noon. Plop down in your pajamas with a sugary bowl of cereal and watch a marathon of classic features, choosing from: The Bugs Bunny/Road Runner Show, X-Men, Superfriends, Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles, Thunder Cats, Gargoyles, or GI Joe.
Presidents Note: If you’ve never experienced this, the President feels bad for you and has made it his job to fix it. Every human should also watch Fraggle Rock, but he digresses.

Title: The Original Eight
Vibe: This adventure brings together the original eight members of the Workman and Garvin families for one shared journey. It's not about the destination as much as the opportunity to travel, laugh, solve problems, and create memories together. Someday, future generations will wish they could have been part of this trip. Today, you have the chance to make it happen.
Why It Matters: Families grow, children become adults, schedules become complicated, and life moves faster every year. Coordinating a trip with eight people isn't easy—and that's exactly why it's worth doing. The greatest family traditions are built through shared experiences, not shared calendars. This adventure celebrates the people who laid the foundation for the Workman Adventure Compound and reminds us that time together is one of life's most valuable gifts.
Mission: The original eight members of the Workman and Garvin families must coordinate, pack up, and travel together two more times.
Presidents Note: The hardest part isn't packing. It's getting everyone's calendar to agree.

Title: Digital Detox
Vibe: Silence has become one of life's rarest experiences.
Why It Matters: Technology has brought incredible convenience, but it's also become a constant distraction. Disconnecting—even for just one day—helps restore focus, strengthen relationships, reduce stress, and remind us that some of life's best moments happen when we're fully present. The WAC has always been about creating memories, and those memories are richer when we're experiencing them instead of documenting them.
Mission: Go a full, consecutive 24 hours with zero digital technology—no phone, no TV, no computer, no iPad, and no MacBook.
Presidents Note: You're probably going to fail this challenge at least once.

Title: A Christmas Story
Vibe: Every childhood has that one toy.
Why It Matters: Growing up teaches us that happiness isn't found in things—but that doesn't make childhood dreams any less meaningful. Looking back helps us appreciate how far we've come while reminding us of the excitement, wonder, and imagination that shaped who we are.
Mission: Reflect on your childhood and identify the one specific toy you desperately wished you had that you never got (think little Ralphie in A Christmas Story). You have two choices to clear this badge: 1. Buy that old toy and share it with members of the WAC so everyone can reminisce, play, and laugh like a child again, OR 2. Buy that toy and donate it directly to other children to enjoy.
Presidents Note: You'll never forget the feeling of finally holding that childhood dream... or watching it become someone else's.

Title: The Millionare Mindset
Vibe: One less unnecessary purchase. One smarter financial choice. One month of consistency repeated over and over again. This challenge isn't about living cheaply—it's about learning that small, intentional decisions can create remarkable results over time.
Why It Matters: Financial freedom is one of the greatest enablers of adventure. Every dollar you intentionally save today becomes another opportunity tomorrow—whether that's traveling with family, retiring earlier, investing in your dreams, or simply reducing stress. Building wealth isn't reserved for the highest earners. It's often the result of ordinary people consistently making extraordinary financial choices.
Mission: Select a consecutive 12-month period and successfully incorporate at least four approved wealth-building habits into your lifestyle. Document the measurable savings and present the results to the WAC Board.
Presidents Note: Nobody accidentally becomes financially successful.

Title: Roots and Branches
Vibe: Some chapters are filled with sacrifice. Others with laughter, traditions, hard work, and unforgettable moments. This adventure challenges you to look beyond names and dates and discover what truly defines your family. Who are you? Where did your values come from? And what part of your story will continue long after you're gone?
Why It Matters: Identity is shaped over generations. Our beliefs, traditions, work ethic, hobbies, and values often begin long before we're born. Taking the time to understand and document those influences strengthens family connections, encourages gratitude, and preserves stories that might otherwise disappear. Just as importantly, it challenges us to consider what legacy we're leaving for the next generation.
Mission: Build a well-thought-out, deeply insightful presentation answering the following 5 fundamental self-discovery questions: 1. How do you define your ethnic, cultural, or national identity? 2. What traditions or rituals do you observe in everyday life or on holidays, and where did they originate? 3. What are your most prized values or hobbies, and did your relatives share them? 4. What positive traits or tendencies describe your family in general, and how do they manifest in you? 5. What specific values or habits do you see manifesting in your children or the younger generation?
Presidents Note: A family's story... only survives if someone takes the time to tell it.

Title: Booked for Adventure
Vibe: Books have the power to transport you across continents, through history, into the minds of remarkable people, and even into worlds that exist only through imagination. This challenge isn't about reading because you have to—it's about discovering ideas that change the way you think, inspire the way you live, and encourage the adventures you'll someday pursue yourself.
Why It Matters: Reading is one of the greatest investments you can make in yourself. Every book offers a chance to learn from someone else's experiences, mistakes, and successes without leaving your chair. Whether you choose biographies, history, adventure, science, or fiction, each completed book expands your perspective and equips you with knowledge that lasts a lifetime. Strong minds build strong leaders, strong families, and extraordinary adventures.
Mission: Read a minimum of five full-length books during a single calendar year and document each one with a brief summary or lesson learned.
Presidents Note: Comic books don't suddenly become "full-length novels" because you stacked twelve of them together.

Title: The Hurt Locker
Vibe: The best emergency is the one you're prepared for. This adventure isn't about becoming a doctor—it's about becoming the person who stays calm when everyone else is panicking.
Why It Matters: First aid is one of the few skills that can literally save a life. Knowing how to stop severe bleeding, recognize environmental emergencies, and provide immediate care until professional help arrives builds confidence, responsibility, and preparedness. While we all hope these skills are never needed, they're too important to learn only after an emergency begins.
Mission: Demonstrate proficiency in lifesaving first aid by correctly applying a tourniquet and pressure dressing, and explaining the proper treatment for choking, burns, hypothermia, and frostbite.
Presidents Note: Let's be honest... if someone says, "Does anybody here know first aid?" that's a question you want to answer with confidence—not by quietly pretending to check your phone.

Title: The Perfect Storm
Vibe: The water has a way of calling us. Whether it's fishing at sunrise, cruising across the lake, swimming on a hot summer day, or simply relaxing on the dock, some of the best WAC memories happen on or around the water. This challenge is about making sure every adventure ends the same way it started—with everyone safely back on shore.
Why It Matters: Water is one of nature's greatest playgrounds, but it also demands respect. Understanding boating safety, rescue principles, life jackets, weather awareness, and emergency response helps protect not only you, but everyone sharing the adventure. A little knowledge today can prevent a tragedy tomorrow, making every day on the water safer and more enjoyable.
Mission: Complete an online water safety course.
Presidents Note: Let's clear something up... just because you've been swimming since you were five doesn't make you an expert in water safety.

Title: Safety Not Guarenteed
Vibe: Knowledge is one of the few things you can carry on every adventure. Whether you're around a campfire, in a tree stand, at the shooting range, or exploring backcountry trails, the right training gives you the confidence to enjoy the experience while keeping yourself and others safe. Every course completed is another tool added to your personal toolbox.
Why It Matters: Adventure and responsibility go hand in hand. Safety education develops good judgment, builds confidence, and prepares you to make smart decisions when they matter most. These aren't just certificates to hang on the wall—they're practical skills that can protect you, your family, and your friends for years to come. Investing in yourself is one of the greatest adventures you'll ever undertake.
Mission: Successfully complete any two approved safety or preparedness courses from the following list: Fire Safety, Range Safety, Self-Defense, Bowhunter Education, Snowmobile Safety, Hunter Safety, or ORV Safety.
Presidents Note: Let's be honest... nobody has ever said, "I really wish I knew less about staying alive."

Title: Man Down
Vibe: This is not hide-and-seek. It is a controlled rescue mission where organization, teamwork, and perseverance determine whether everyone returns to the staging area together.
Why It Matters: Real emergencies rarely occur beside a paved road with perfect lighting and an ambulance parked nearby. Search and rescue situations require people who can remain calm, organize limited resources, communicate effectively, recognize life-threatening injuries, and move someone through difficult terrain. This challenge builds leadership, teamwork, casualty-assessment skills, physical confidence, and an understanding that rescue does not end when the missing person is found—it ends when that person reaches safety.
Mission: Locate a hidden simulated casualty within a dense wooded search area using an organized grid or sector-search method. Conduct an immediate casualty assessment and tactical triage, then safely transport the casualty at least 100 yards to the designated staging area using an appropriate carry or improvised litter.
Presidents Note: Finding the casualty is the easy part; discovering that your ‘victim’ weighs 240 pounds is where the real adventure begins.

Title: Thunderstruck
Vibe: At the WAC, watching the weather isn't just about deciding whether to build a campfire. It's about understanding the sky well enough to recognize danger before it arrives. Whether protecting family, fellow members, or neighbors, you'll become someone others can trust when the weather turns serious.
Why It Matters: Modern forecasting is powerful, but trained spotters continue to play a critical role in severe weather detection. Accurate ground reports help the National Weather Service verify radar signatures, improve warning accuracy, and provide communities with valuable minutes of advance notice. Completing SKYWARN training also develops greater awareness of weather hazards, emergency preparedness, and outdoor risk management—skills that benefit every adventure at the WAC.
Mission: Successfully complete the official NOAA/National Weather Service SKYWARN® Spotter training course and register your official spotter identification with the WAC compound board.
Presidents Note: If your weather forecast is 'looks kinda cloudy,' it's time to take the course again.

Title: Bark Ranger
Vibe: Brandy isn't just the WAC dog—she's a permanent member of the family, a trail companion, an unofficial greeter, and a professional expert at finding the worst-smelling thing in the woods to roll in. Spending time with her means muddy paws, endless affection, and more dog hair than you thought was physically possible.
Why It Matters: Dogs have been trusted outdoor companions for thousands of years, providing companionship, security, and an unmatched enthusiasm for every adventure. Spending time caring for Brandy reinforces responsibility, patience, and the simple joy of sharing the outdoors with a loyal companion. Along the way, members also discover an important WAC truth: every memorable adventure is a little better when a dog is part of it.
Mission: Complete all of the following: Take Brandy on a walk of at least one continuous mile. Bathe Brandy after she has inevitably rolled in something disgusting while at the WAC. Allow Brandy to sleep in your bed for one full night.
Presidents Note: If Brandy rolls in something disgusting twice during this challenge... that's called extra credit.

Title: Hakuna Frittata
Vibe: Packed with protein and requiring only a fraction of the land, water, and feed needed for traditional livestock, edible insects have sustained cultures for thousands of years. At the WAC, this challenge isn't about grossing people out. It's about embracing curiosity, expanding your culinary horizons, and proving you're willing to step outside your comfort zone.
Why It Matters: Self-reliance includes the willingness to adapt. Throughout history, people have survived by learning to use the resources available to them, and edible insects remain one of the most sustainable and nutrient-dense protein sources on Earth. This challenge encourages open-mindedness, food safety awareness, and an appreciation for cultures whose traditional diets may differ from our own. Sometimes growth begins where comfort ends.
Mission: Successfully cultivate or obtain a verified source of edible crickets, mealworms, or another WAC Board-approved insect protein. Clean, safely prepare, and incorporate the insects into a meal that is consumed by the participant.
Presidents Note: The first bug is the hardest. After that... they're all equally weird.

Title: The Molar Medal
Vibe: Some adventures leave you with a great story. Others leave you with a scar. This one... leaves you with a dental bill.
Why It Matters: The WAC has always been about embracing adventure, and adventure occasionally comes with a few bumps, bruises, and unforgettable stories. While safety should always come first, this badge serves as a humorous reminder that the best memories aren't always the ones that go according to plan. Sometimes the stories worth telling begin with the words, "You're not going to believe what happened..."
Mission: Must require dental work as a result of an experience at the WAC
Presidents Note: Let's make one thing perfectly clear... this is absolutely not a challenge you should actively pursue.

Title: The Cost of Adventure
Vibe: Adventure isn't always clean. Sometimes the trail wins, the tree doesn't move, gravity reminds you who's in charge, or a perfectly good idea ends with someone saying, "Well... that didn't go as planned."
Why It Matters: Every great adventurer eventually learns that risk and responsibility go hand in hand. While the WAC encourages exploration, competition, and trying new things, it also values preparation, good judgment, and looking out for one another. This badge doesn't celebrate getting hurt—it celebrates resilience, recovery, and the stories that become part of WAC history.
Mission: Sustain a legitimate physical injury requiring medical care from an experience at the WAC
Presidents Note: If you're reading this thinking, "I wonder how I can earn this one," you've already failed the common-sense portion of the adventure.

Title: Venom & Valor
Vibe: Whether it was a snake, spider, tick, dog, raccoon, bee, wasp, or some other creature that clearly misunderstood your intentions, you've officially experienced one of the less enjoyable sides of outdoor adventure. This isn't about bad luck—it's about earning a story that usually begins with, "I never even saw it..."
