Why It Matters: Exploring the outdoors means sharing it with wildlife, and occasionally those encounters don't go according to plan. This badge reminds us that awareness, preparation, and respect for nature are just as important as courage. While no one hopes to need medical treatment after a bite or sting, knowing how to respond—and learning from the experience—is part of becoming a better outdoorsman.
Mission: Must require medical treatment specifically from being bitten by something.
Presidents Note: Mosquitoes don't count.

Title: Six Feet Apart
Vibe: History isn't always found in textbooks—sometimes you live through it. This adventure recognizes a moment when the entire world changed almost overnight. Empty streets, face masks, canceled gatherings, video calls, and uncertainty became part of everyday life. Whether you remember it as challenging, frustrating, or simply strange, it's a reminder that even difficult times eventually become part of history.
Why It Matters: Living through a global health crisis teaches resilience, adaptability, and the importance of family, community, and preparedness. It reminds us that life can change quickly, but it also demonstrates how people find ways to support one another, adapt, and move forward. Every generation has defining moments, and this is one that many will remember for the rest of their lives.
Mission: Live through a global health crisis.
Presidents Note: There aren't many adventures where staying home was considered doing your part.

Title: Eyes to the Sky
Vibe: When the sun disappears and the last campfire embers begin to glow, an entirely different world comes alive overhead.
Why It Matters: Modern life rarely gives us the opportunity to experience genuine darkness. Visiting Michigan's Dark Sky Preserves encourages us to disconnect from artificial light, appreciate the beauty of the natural world, and develop a deeper understanding of astronomy and conservation. Sometimes the greatest adventure isn't found by moving—it begins the moment you stop, look skyward, and simply observe.
Mission: Visit and spend time stargazing at any two of Michigan's designated Dark Sky Preserves: Lake Hudson Recreation Area, Negwegon State Park, Port Crescent State Park, Rockport Recreation Area, Thompson's Harbor State Park, or Wilderness State Park.
Presidents Note: Put your phone away.

Title: The Grocery Store is Closed
Vibe: Hidden among the leaves are berries, mushrooms, greens, roots, and edible plants that have fed people for thousands of years. This adventure challenges you to slow down, sharpen your observation skills, and discover that Michigan's forests are filled with nature's pantry—if you know where to look.
Why It Matters: Learning to identify edible wild plants and fungi builds awareness, patience, and respect for the natural world. It encourages careful observation and an appreciation for traditional outdoor knowledge passed down through generations. More importantly, it teaches an important lesson: never eat what you cannot positively identify. Responsible foraging is as much about knowing what to leave behind as it is about knowing what you can safely gather.
Mission: Successfully locate, positively identify, and document five different wild edible plants or fungi native to Michigan. Identification should be verified using a reliable field guide, experienced mentor, or recognized identification resource.
Presidents Note: Just because it looks delicious doesn't mean it's invited to dinner.

Title: Room Service Not Included
Vibe: Trade the walls for canvas and the ceiling for a sky full of stars. Leave behind the comforts of home and embrace a weekend where the campfire becomes the living room, birds replace alarm clocks, and every meal somehow tastes better outdoors.
Why It Matters: Camping reconnects us with the outdoors in a way that cabins and campers simply can't. Pitching a tent, cooking over a fire, and sleeping beneath the trees encourages self-reliance, appreciation for nature, and a slower pace of life. Michigan's state parks are some of the state's greatest treasures, and this adventure is an invitation to explore them one campsite at a time.
Mission: Camp at one of Michigan’s State Parks. You must use a tent. For a minimum duration of 2 days.
Presidents Note: Let's settle one debate right now... sleeping in a forty-foot motorhome with air conditioning, satellite TV, and a recliner is called vacationing. Sleeping in a tent while wondering what made that noise outside at 2:00 in the morning... that's camping.

Title: Indiana Bones
Vibe: Long before GPS, smartphones, and highways, someone stood exactly where you're standing today. They worked the land, traveled forgotten roads, built homes, fought wars, or simply dropped something they'll never know you found. This adventure invites you to become a modern-day treasure hunter, uncovering pieces of history that have quietly waited beneath leaves, soil, and time.
Why It Matters: Relic hunting encourages curiosity, patience, research, and respect for history. Whether using a metal detector or simply exploring forgotten places, you'll begin to see the landscape differently. Every discovery offers a chance to learn about the people who came before us while reinforcing the importance of preserving historical artifacts responsibly and following all applicable laws and property rights.
Mission: Use a metal detector, shovel, or sheer wilderness exploration to locate, dig up, and identify a tangible historical artifact from a long-gone era.
Presidents Note: You're probably not going to find Blackbeard's treasure.

Title: Rock Bottom
Vibe: Adventure isn't always found on the highest mountain.
Why It Matters: Underground exploration reveals a side of Michigan that many people never realize exists. These hidden places showcase fascinating geology, mining history, and delicate ecosystems that have remained largely unchanged for centuries. Exploring them encourages curiosity, respect for nature, and an appreciation for the forces that shaped the land long before people arrived.
Mission: Explore a recognized Michigan cave, underground mine, or other natural underground formation. Document the experience by photographing unique geological features, historical remnants, or native wildlife encountered during the adventure.
Presidents Note: Every cave is colder than you expected.

Title: Cache Me if You Can
Vibe: Adventure is hiding in plain sight—you just have to know where to look. Geocaching combines hiking, exploration, problem-solving, and a little treasure hunting into one unforgettable experience. Grab a GPS or your phone, follow the coordinates, and discover hidden caches that thousands of people walk past every day without ever knowing they're there.
Why It Matters: Geocaching encourages curiosity, exploration, and an appreciation for places you might otherwise overlook. It turns an ordinary hike into an adventure, teaches navigation and observation skills, and reminds us that the journey can be just as rewarding as the destination. Every cache has a story, and every find adds another memory to your own adventure.
Mission: Successfully locate, sign the logbook, and document at least three official geocaches listed on the Geocaching® network.
Presidents Note: Here's the deal... this isn't National Treasure. You probably won't uncover pirate gold, the Lost City of Atlantis, or Jimmy Hoffa. More often than not, you'll find a waterproof container tucked under a log, hidden in a hollow tree, or disguised as something you'd normally walk right past.

Title: Rock Bottom
Vibe: One wave may uncover a 350-million-year-old fossil coral. Another might reveal a brilliant piece of blue industrial history. Somewhere nearby, a colorful agate or jasper has been waiting through countless ice ages for someone to notice it. Grab a bucket, keep your eyes down, and let the hunt begin.
Why It Matters: Rockhounding teaches patience, observation, and an appreciation for Michigan's incredible geological history. Every specimen tells a different story—from ancient tropical seas that created Petoskey stones to the 19th-century iron smelting that produced Leland Blue. The search itself is often just as rewarding as the discovery, turning an ordinary walk along the shoreline into a modern-day treasure hunt. When collecting, always follow local regulations and remember that Michigan limits recreational collection on state-owned lands to 25 pounds of rocks, minerals, and fossils per person per year, with additional restrictions in some locations.
Mission: Search a Michigan shoreline or glacial gravel deposit and successfully locate three distinct geological specimens: one Petoskey Stone, one Leland Blue (historical slag), and one unique agate or jasper.
Presidents Note: At some point, you're going to convince yourself that every single gray rock is a Petoskey stone.

Title: Leaf Me Alone
Vibe: Once you learn to tell an oak from a maple, a beech from a birch, or a hickory from an ash, the forest begins to tell a completely different story. Then, just as winter loosens its grip, you'll tap one of those maples and watch clear sap slowly drip into a bucket before being transformed into one of Michigan's sweetest traditions.
Why It Matters: Learning to identify trees changes the way you experience the outdoors. Instead of seeing one green blur, you begin recognizing the species that provide wildlife habitat, firewood, shade, food, and even syrup. Participating in maple syrup production connects you to a centuries-old tradition that rewards patience, hard work, and an appreciation for one of Michigan's most iconic natural resources.
Mission: 1. Successfully identify and label 10 different native Michigan tree species found on or immediately adjacent to the WAC property. 2. Tap a maple tree and participate in collecting and boiling sap into maple syrup.
Presidents Note: It takes around 40 gallons of sap to make one gallon of syrup.

Title: Where the Wild Things Are
Vibe: Some adventures aren't measured in miles—they're measured in moments. The excitement of spotting a moose in the morning fog, watching a bald eagle soar overhead, or catching your first glimpse of a whale breaking the surface is something you'll never forget.
Why It Matters: Wild animals remind us that there are still places where nature operates on its own terms. Seeking out these incredible species encourages travel, patience, conservation, and a deeper appreciation for the ecosystems they call home. Every sighting is a reminder that the world is still full of places capable of inspiring awe, if we're willing to go looking for them.
Mission: Find a way to see six of these fantastic animals in the wild: Moose, Grey Wolf, Whale, Brown, Bear, Elk, Gemsbok, Key Deer, Caribou, Bison, Big Horn Sheep, Alligator, Bald Eagle, Manatee, Panther/Cougar
Presidents Note: Let's get one thing straight... seeing wildlife and chasing wildlife are two very different things. If your plan involves running after a moose for a better selfie, you've officially failed the spirit of this adventure... and possibly your survival instincts.

Title: Aliens Optional
Vibe: Look up... because the greatest show on Earth isn't always on Earth.
Why It Matters: The night sky has inspired explorers, scientists, and storytellers for thousands of years. Taking the time to witness these celestial events reminds us how vast the universe truly is and how fortunate we are to experience it. Each observation is another opportunity to slow down, look beyond our everyday lives, and appreciate the incredible world—and universe—we're a part of.
Mission: Successfully view all of the following: eclipse, meteor shower, norther lights, comet, the international space station, jupiter, the orion nebula.
Presidents Note: Here's the good news... you don't need a billion-dollar telescope or your own space program to complete this adventure. You just need curiosity, a little patience, and the willingness to stay awake later than you probably should.

Title: Not All Who Wander
Vibe: Michigan is more than highways, small towns, and familiar campgrounds. From towering pines and rugged trails to quiet rivers and breathtaking overlooks, some of the state's greatest adventures are waiting in places you've never been.
Why It Matters: Adventure begins the moment you leave your comfort zone. Exploring new forests and parks encourages curiosity, appreciation for public lands, and a deeper connection to the incredible natural resources that make Michigan unique. Every new destination offers different landscapes, wildlife, and memories that simply can't be found by visiting the same places year after year.
Mission: Physically travel to and explore three separate Michigan State Forests or National Parks that you have never visited before.
Presidents Note: Let's be honest... it's easy to keep going back to your favorite campground, favorite trail, or favorite fishing spot. There's nothing wrong with that... but this adventure isn't about what's familiar.

Title: Because FREEDOM Bitch!!
Vibe: This adventure celebrates the sights, sounds, flavors, and traditions that have become part of the American experience. It's loud, it's fun, it's unapologetically patriotic, and it's best enjoyed surrounded by family and friends. Spend one weekend embracing the simple pleasures that have brought people together across generations.
Why It Matters: America is a blend of traditions, cultures, and communities, but there are a few experiences that have become nearly universal: gathering around a grill, cheering on a football game, spending time outdoors, enjoying good food, and celebrating the freedoms that allow us to do so. This adventure isn't about perfection—it's about appreciating those moments that make weekends with family and friends memorable.
Mission: Complete all 9 of the most American tasks imaginable over the span of a single weekend: 1. Shoot guns—and lots of them (at least 3 different firearms, and one must be an AR). 2. Ride directly in the back of a pickup truck. 3. Eat a slice of classic apple pie. 4. Drink cold beer. 5. Grill up a massive steak. 6. Blast Kid Rock on the speakers. 7. Watch a football game (and absolutely none of that soccer shit either!). 8. Light up a massive bonfire. 9. Stand up and sing the National Anthem.
Presidents Note: This might be the easiest badge to convince your friends to complete.

Title: Animal House
Vibe: For one night, age is just a number... and dignity is entirely optional.
Why It Matters: Life has a way of convincing us to become too serious. A toga party reminds us that some of our best memories come from doing something completely out of the ordinary with the people we care about. Laughter strengthens friendships, creates traditions, and proves that adventure doesn't always require traveling somewhere new—it sometimes begins with a bedsheet and a willingness to look absolutely ridiculous.
Mission: Physically attend the designated compound gathering, wrap yourself entirely in a bedsheet toga, and party like a wild youth whose joints aren't currently screaming.
Presidents Note: Growing older is mandatory. Growing up has always been optional.

Title: Weekend Warriors
Vibe: Whether you're defending a fort, flanking through the woods, or making one heroic charge that everyone will laugh about later, paintball is the perfect mix of strategy, teamwork, and controlled chaos. This adventure isn't about being the fastest trigger—it's about working together, thinking ahead, and embracing the adrenaline that comes with every match.
Why It Matters: Paintball rewards communication, leadership, teamwork, and problem-solving under pressure. Every match teaches players to trust one another, adapt to changing situations, and stay calm when things don't go according to plan. Most importantly, it creates unforgettable stories, friendly rivalries, and memories that will be retold around the campfire for years.
Mission: Participate in an organized paintball competition by either competing in an official WAC tournament on compound grounds or representing the WAC in an off-site paintball event.
Presidents Note: Everybody has a brilliant battle plan... until the first paintball flies past their head.

Title: Shake, Stir, and Serve
Vibe: Whether it's something refreshing after a long day in the woods, a warm drink around the campfire, or a creative concoction that nobody saw coming, this adventure challenges you to become the WAC's resident beverage inventor. Experiment, taste, adjust, and create something uniquely your own. Who knows... your recipe might become the next WAC tradition.
Why It Matters: Creativity isn't limited to building projects or outdoor adventures—it belongs in the kitchen too. Developing an original drink encourages experimentation, attention to detail, and the confidence to create something from scratch. Sharing your creation with others turns a simple recipe into a shared experience, proving that some of the best memories begin with the words, "You've got to try this."
Mission: Create two original signature beverage recipes, source the ingredients, and prepare and serve them to WAC members for tasting and judging.
Presidents Note: Don't just pour random ingredients into a glass and call it "artisan."

Title: The War Room
Vibe: Empires rise and fall. Alliances are formed and broken. Diseases spread, railroads expand, fortunes are won and lost, and lifelong rivalries begin over a single misplaced move. This challenge celebrates strategy, patience, competition, and the simple joy of gathering around a table with family and friends.
Why It Matters: Strategy games teach far more than how to win. They encourage critical thinking, long-term planning, teamwork, communication, adaptability, and sportsmanship. More importantly, they create an excuse to disconnect from screens and reconnect with one another. Every completed game becomes another evening filled with laughter, friendly competition, and stories that often become just as memorable as outdoor adventures.
Mission: Complete each of the ten designated strategy games to completion with fellow WAC members. In addition, research, present, and play two new strategy games for consideration as future WAC traditions. 1. Settlers of Catan, 2. Pandemic, 3. Risk, 4. Stratego, 5. Clue, 6. Ticket to Ride, 7. Monopoly, 8. Feudal, 9. Chess, 10. Checkers. You must also presnet two new strategy games to the WAC for game night.
Presidents Note: You don't get to quit just because you're losing.

Title: Going Live
Vibe: Not everyone can make every campfire, tournament, bonfire, or ridiculous moment that unfolds at the WAC. Going live lets those who are miles away still laugh, cheer, and feel like they're part of the adventure.
Why It Matters: The WAC has never been just about a place—it's about the people who share it. Livestreaming special moments helps keep family and friends connected, preserves memories, and allows everyone to experience the adventure together, even when life keeps them somewhere else.
Mission: Livestream an event at the WAC
Presidents Note: Capture the laughter, the competition, the campfire stories, the fish that "was definitely this big," and maybe even Uncle Joe falling out of a lawn chair. Those are the moments worth sharing. Just remember one thing—put the phone down once in a while. The goal isn't to watch the WAC through a screen... it's to make everyone watching wish they were standing right there with you.

Title: Picture Perfect
Vibe: What begins as a pile of seemingly random cardboard slowly transforms into a complete picture through patience, teamwork, and determination. There are no shortcuts, no lucky breaks, and no instant victories. Just good company, great conversations, and the satisfaction of watching order emerge from chaos.
Why It Matters: Jigsaw puzzles encourage patience, problem-solving, communication, and perseverance. They remind us that meaningful accomplishments often require many small victories instead of one giant leap. More importantly, puzzles have a unique way of bringing people together. The conversations that happen around the table often become just as memorable as finishing the puzzle itself.
Mission: Gather family or friends and successfully complete a jigsaw puzzle containing at least 1,000 pieces from start to finish.
Presidents Note: No missing pieces allowed at the finish line. You must preserve the completed puzzle or take a verified group photo with it for log approval.

Title: Passport to the Table
Vibe: Forget takeout and frozen pizza for one night. This adventure is your passport to another part of the world without ever leaving the WAC. Pick a country, embrace its traditions, and create an evening filled with authentic food, culture, and a little curiosity.
Why It Matters: Food has a unique way of bringing people together. Sharing a traditional meal opens the door to learning about different cultures, customs, and histories while creating lasting memories around the table. This adventure reminds us that understanding the world often begins with simply sitting down and breaking bread with others—even if you're doing it at the WAC.
Mission: Select a country and deliver a complete cultural dinner spread from that country to the members of the WAC.
Presidents Note: Let's make one thing clear... putting pineapple on a frozen pizza does not count as experiencing Hawaiian cuisine. Nice try.

Title: Ride the Lightning
Vibe: Some adventures are about comfort. This isn't one of them. Taking a Taser exposure is a rite of passage for many law enforcement officers and instructors—a brief but unforgettable lesson in controlled discomfort.
Why It Matters: Stepping outside your comfort zone builds confidence, resilience, and respect for experiences you may never fully understand otherwise. Whether you're in law enforcement, training for a career, or simply accepting a unique challenge, this adventure is about facing something intimidating with courage and good humor. Sometimes the best stories begin with the words, "Well... this might hurt."
Mission: Successfully take a charge from the Taser.
Presidents Note: Let's get one thing straight—this is not an invitation to buy a Taser on the internet and start shocking each other behind the barn. If that's your plan, congratulations... you've completely missed the point.

Title: Dead Men Tell No Tales
Vibe: For one evening, leave the real world behind and step into a story where everyone has a role to play. Dress the part, embrace the mystery, and prepare for an evening filled with suspicious alibis, unexpected twists, terrible accents, and plenty of laughs. Whether you're the detective, the victim, or the prime suspect, the only thing guaranteed is that someone is getting accused.
Why It Matters: The best memories aren't always made on trails or around campfires. Sometimes they're created around a dinner table where friends commit fully to being ridiculous together. A murder mystery dinner encourages teamwork, creativity, problem-solving, and plenty of laughter while giving everyone a chance to step outside their everyday routine and become part of the story.
Mission: Attend or host a murder mystery dinner with other WAC members
Presidents Note: The goal isn't to solve the mystery faster than everyone else. The goal is to laugh, play along, and create one of those nights that people are still talking about years later. Because at the WAC, a great story is often worth more than solving the case.

Title: Pixel Perfect
Vibe: This challenge takes you back to the golden age of gaming, when every level had to be earned, every victory came through practice, and your reputation was measured by the initials at the top of the high-score screen. Dust off the classics, grab a controller, and prove you've still got what it takes.
Why It Matters: Retro games rewarded perseverance, quick thinking, and determination rather than tutorials and unlimited retries. They challenged players to learn through failure, improve through repetition, and celebrate every hard-earned victory. More importantly, they created countless memories shared with friends and family gathered around one television.
Mission: Achieve a verified high score or complete a full play-through of three classic retro video games from the arcade, NES, Sega Genesis, or Super Nintendo era without the use of cheat codes, save states, or modern gameplay assists.
Presidents Note: The graphics may have been simple, but the memories were in high definition.

Title: The Belly of the Beast
Vibe: Some people climb mountains. Some people run marathons. At the WAC... sometimes we stare down an unreasonable amount of food and convince ourselves, "Yeah... I can finish that."
Why It Matters: The WAC has always been about creating unforgettable experiences that people laugh about for years. Food challenges aren't really about eating—they're about friendly competition, cheering each other on, and watching someone confidently underestimate just how much food they're about to consume. Success is memorable, but spectacular failure is often even more entertaining.
Mission: Successfully complete an extreme food challenge at the WAC.
Presidents Note: Choose a challenge that's outrageous enough to make everyone laugh, but safe enough that your biggest regret is agreeing to it in the first place.

Title: Cowboy Up
Vibe: You've watched the show. You've admired the grit, the loyalty, and the no-nonsense way of life. Now it's your turn to step into the boots of a modern-day cowboy—or at least fake it convincingly for a weekend.
Why It Matters: The Duttons remind us that life isn't always comfortable, and the people you love are worth protecting. While we may not own the Yellowstone Ranch, we can still appreciate the values it represents: loyalty, hard work, courage, and standing by family and friends when it matters most. This adventure is less about pretending to be a cowboy and more about embracing the mindset that comes with it.
Mission: Live like a Dutton, complete two of the following: go skinny dipping, attend a rodeo, ride a horse, attend a cattle auction, take someone to the train station, protect a family member in a fight.
Presidents Note: Let's be honest... after watching Yellowstone, about half of America suddenly wanted to own a ranch, wear cowboy hats, and squint dramatically into the sunset.

Title: Not Really Fake News
Vibe: Whether you're interviewing family members, snapping photos, filming the action, writing headlines, or filling the pages of the official WAC journal, this challenge transforms you into the storyteller of the weekend. Your mission isn't just to report what happened—it's to preserve the moments, laughter, traditions, and memories that future generations will wish they could have experienced.
Why It Matters: Memories fade. Stories evolve. But a written journal, newsletter, photograph, or video can preserve a moment exactly as it happened. By documenting WAC adventures, you become the family's historian, ensuring that today's campfires, competitions, and celebrations remain part of tomorrow's legacy. Long after the weekend is over, your work will allow others to relive the adventure.
Mission: Serve as the official WAC correspondent by documenting a major event through one of the following: a printed newsletter, digital magazine, video news report, photo essay, or official WAC journal entry. Publish or archive your work as part of the permanent WAC record.
Presidents Note: The people making history rarely realize they're making history.

Title: Picture This
Vibe: Armed with nothing but a camera, a checklist, and a ticking clock, your team will race across the WAC in search of the strange, the unexpected, and the downright ridiculous. Every photo tells a story, every clue sparks a new adventure, and every minute counts. Strategy matters just as much as speed, because the best teams don't just run—they think.
Why It Matters: A great scavenger hunt encourages teamwork, communication, observation, creativity, and quick decision-making. It challenges players to look at familiar places in entirely new ways while creating hilarious memories that often become more memorable than the competition itself. The best part isn't crossing the finish line—it's laughing at the photos afterward.
Mission: Form a team and capture photographic proof of at least 15 items from the official WAC Scavenger List within a three-hour time limit. All photos must be taken during the challenge and verified by the WAC Board.
Presidents Note: Every ordinary object suddenly becomes suspicious.

Title: Professionally Questionable
Vibe: Whether you become a certified hot sauce taster, meteorologist for a day, dinosaur expert, whiskey steward, cheese connoisseur, citizen scientist, or complete some other delightfully obscure training, this challenge celebrates curiosity and the joy of learning something unexpected.
Why It Matters: Learning doesn't end with school or your career. The most interesting people are often the ones who never stop exploring new ideas. Pursuing an unusual certification encourages curiosity, broadens your perspective, and reminds us that knowledge can be both practical and entertaining. Sometimes the most memorable lessons are the ones nobody ever expected to learn.
Mission: Successfully complete and earn an unusual, obscure, humorous, or fascinating certification, license, or training credential. Present what you learned to the WAC membership.
Presidents Note: Around the WAC, we believe the most interesting adventures often begin with one simple question..."I wonder if there is a class for that"

Title: The Compound Classic
Vibe: The WAC Decathlon isn't about being the strongest, fastest, or most athletic person on the property. It's about showing up, competing in every event, and embracing the friendly rivalries that make weekends at the WAC unforgettable. Some events reward skill, others reward luck, and a few may simply reward whoever has the least embarrassing performance.
Why It Matters: Competition has always brought people together. Whether you're chasing first place or simply trying not to finish last, playing games with friends and family creates memories that last far longer than the final scores. The WAC Decathlon celebrates sportsmanship, perseverance, and the simple joy of spending an entire weekend outdoors laughing, competing, and cheering each other on.
Mission: Compete in a single weekend covering ten distinct events: 1. Horseshoes, 2. Cornhole, 3. Darts, 4. Frisbee Can Toss, 5. Billiards, 6. Ladder Golf, 7. Washers, 8. Target Shooting, 9. Axe Throwing, and 10. KanJam.
Presidents Note: Let's clear something up before we begin... this isn't the Olympics. Nobody's getting a million-dollar endorsement deal because they dominated the washers tournament.

Title: Scare Tactics
Vibe: Halloween is one of the few times of year when grown adults are encouraged to dress ridiculously, tell ghost stories, carve pumpkins, and willingly walk into places designed to scare them. This adventure celebrates the fun side of fear by combining a spine-tingling haunted attraction with a Halloween gathering where memories are made one laugh—and one jump scare—at a time.
Why It Matters: Traditions are what give each season its personality. Halloween encourages creativity, imagination, laughter, and a little courage while bringing people together through costumes, decorations, and shared experiences. Whether you're screaming your way through a haunted house or hosting friends around a fire with cider and candy, the goal is the same: create memories worth talking about long after October ends.
Mission: Celebrate Halloween by completing both of the following: Attend a professionally operated haunted attraction. Host a Halloween party or themed gathering for family, friends, or WAC members.
Presidents Note: It's about laughing so hard that nobody remembers who screamed first.

Title: No Filibusters Allowed
Vibe: The WAC Handbook isn't just a collection of rules—it's the story of how the compound grows, adapts, and preserves its traditions. This challenge invites you to take a seat at the table, debate ideas, improve the organization, and leave your mark on the future of the WAC.
Why It Matters: Traditions survive because each generation chooses to protect and improve them. Thoughtful discussion, respectful debate, and collaborative decision-making ensure that the WAC remains organized, welcoming, and relevant for years to come. By helping shape the Handbook, you become more than a participant—you become a steward of the compound's future.
Mission: Participate in an official legislative session of the WAC by proposing, debating, drafting, revising, or ratifying a constitutional amendment, policy, or Code of Conduct that becomes part of the official WAC Handbook.
Presidents Note: Not every idea deserves to become a rule.

Title: Officially Overdressed
Vibe: Every great adventure deserves a great shirt. Whether it's hilarious, patriotic, inspired by an inside joke, or designed to commemorate an unforgettable weekend, this is your chance to leave your mark on WAC history.
Why It Matters: A T-shirt is more than fabric and ink—it's a memory you can wear. The best shirts instantly bring back stories, inside jokes, and adventures shared with friends and family. This challenge encourages creativity while helping preserve the unique traditions and personalities that make the WAC unlike anywhere else.
Mission: Design a t-shirt, get it made, and wear it at the WAC or other travel event.
Presidents Note: Here's the challenge... don't make another boring T-shirt with your name on the front and a clip-art pine tree slapped on the back. If you're going to create a WAC shirt, make it something people actually want to wear.

Title: Where Are My Keys?
Vibe: The call comes without warning. No planning, no second chances, and no time to overthink it. You've got thirty minutes to grab your gear, load the vehicle, and head for the WAC. Once you arrive, you'll find out whether you packed like an adventurer... or like someone heading to the grocery store. This challenge is all about thinking ahead before the clock ever starts.
Why It Matters: Preparedness isn't something you can fake when time is limited. Whether it's a camping trip, a family emergency, or simply an unexpected opportunity to escape for the weekend, being able to organize yourself quickly is a valuable life skill. This adventure develops planning, prioritization, and self-reliance while proving that the best time to prepare is before you're asked to.
Mission: Required to drop everything, upon a random alarm, and travel to the WAC. You have thirty minutes to prep supplies and hit the road. Once at the WAC you must be able to explain what you packed and why.
Presidents Note: One day your phone rings, the alarm sounds, or someone announces, "You've got thirty minutes!" Suddenly every decision matters. Do you grab the fishing poles? Extra socks? Coffee? The cast-iron skillet? Did you remember your sleeping bag... or are you planning to explain to everyone why you're wrapped in a beach towel at midnight?

Title: Exit Strategy
Vibe: Hope for the best. Prepare for the unexpected.
Why It Matters: Preparedness isn't about expecting disaster—it's about reducing stress when life throws you a curveball. Building a bug-out bag teaches planning, organization, and critical thinking while ensuring you're ready for emergencies both big and small. The confidence that comes from knowing you're prepared is often more valuable than the gear itself.
Mission: Pack a comprehensive bug-out bag containing 72 hours of survival essentials.
Presidents Note: Let's get one thing straight... a bug-out bag isn't just a backpack full of beef jerky, duct tape, and enough knives to invade a small country.

Title: Night Shift
Vibe: When the sun goes down, the adventure changes. Familiar trails become unfamiliar, simple tasks require patience, and your senses begin to take over. This challenge strips away one of the conveniences we rely on most—artificial light—and replaces it with red lenses, moonlight, campfires, and careful planning. It's amazing how capable you become when you learn to work with the night instead of fighting against it.
Why It Matters: Darkness has a way of exposing weaknesses in both equipment and preparation. This challenge develops confidence, situational awareness, adaptability, and an appreciation for skills that were once second nature. Whether you're camping, hunting, responding to an emergency, or simply enjoying the outdoors after sunset, learning to function without white light teaches patience, discipline, and resourcefulness while revealing just how dependent we've become on flipping a switch.
Mission: Spend a continuous 12-hour overnight period operating the WAC under complete light discipline. Perform normal camp duties and prepare all meals using no white light.
Presidents Note: First things first... your cell phone flashlight is not your friend tonight. In fact, if I catch you accidentally turning it on, expect to hear someone immediately yell, "WHITE LIGHT!" loud enough for the entire county to know you blew the mission.

Title: Cast Away
Vibe: A carefully arranged signal made from rocks, logs, or brightly contrasting materials can turn an empty clearing into a message visible from hundreds of feet in the air. Add a well-managed smoke signal or signal fire, and you've transformed the wilderness into your own emergency beacon. This challenge takes you back to a time before satellite messengers and cell phones, when knowledge, creativity, and preparation were often the only lifelines available.
Why It Matters: Signaling is one of the most overlooked survival skills. Search-and-rescue teams can only assist those they can locate, and a properly constructed ground signal or smoke column dramatically increases the chance of being spotted from the air. Learning these techniques develops problem-solving, resourcefulness, and emergency preparedness while reinforcing a critical survival principle: your goal isn't simply to endure—it's to be found.
Mission: Construct a standardized ground-to-air emergency signal (such as an SOS, X, or other internationally recognized distress marker) that is clearly visible from approximately 500 feet above ground. Then safely demonstrate an emergency smoke or signal fire suitable for daytime or nighttime rescue signaling.
Presidents Note: If the helicopter lands because of your signal... that's success. If the fire department shows up instead... that's a different achievement.

Title: Lost in Space
Vibe: Mars is a long way from the WAC... but survival starts with the same mindset. This challenge is about thinking like an explorer, solving problems with limited resources, and proving that ingenuity often matters more than fancy equipment. Every task is a reminder that humans have always survived by adapting, improvising, and refusing to quit.
Why It Matters: Resourcefulness is one of the greatest survival skills a person can develop. Growing food, generating your own power, conserving water, and learning to live comfortably with fewer conveniences builds confidence and self-reliance. While you're not stranded on another planet, the lessons learned from this challenge are surprisingly practical here on Earth.
Mission: Recreate key survival challenges from The Martian: grow potatoes, generate power with solar energy, collect or reclaim water from an unconventional source, and go without a shower for three consecutive days.
Presidents Note: If you've seen The Martian, you already know the secret to survival isn't luck... it's refusing to give up when Plan A falls apart.

Title: Un-Naked and Afraid
Vibe: Every decision suddenly matters. Shelter must be earned. Fire becomes priceless. Water isn't simply opened—it must be found, purified, and protected. The forest quickly reminds you that comfort is a luxury and confidence comes from preparation, not possessions.
Why It Matters: Primitive survival teaches resourcefulness, patience, and calm decision-making under limited conditions. Restricting equipment forces participants to rely on knowledge instead of gear, strengthening essential outdoor skills such as shelter construction, fire building, water purification, risk management, and environmental awareness. More importantly, the challenge reinforces one of the WAC's core beliefs: true self-reliance comes from what you know—not what you carry.
Mission: Spend 24 continuous hours participating in a planned primitive survival exercise within the WAC woods using only: One fixed-blade knife, One ferrocerium (fire-steel) rod, One metal container suitable for boiling water. The participant's location and expected return time must be known by the WAC President or another designated member before beginning the challenge.
Presidents Note: By Hour 18, that log starts looking suspiciously like a Tempur-Pedic mattress.

Title: In the Heat of the Night
Vibe: For thousands of years, a single ember meant the difference between comfort and hardship, hot meals and cold nights, survival and surrender.
Why It Matters: Fire provides warmth, safe drinking water, cooked food, light, signaling capability, and comfort. Learning to create it without modern conveniences develops perseverance, fine motor skills, problem-solving, and confidence under pressure. More importantly, primitive fire making reinforces a core principle of bushcraft: knowledge and determination often outweigh expensive equipment. When you can create fire from simple materials, you've mastered one of humanity's oldest and most valuable survival skills.
Mission: Successfully ignite a sustainable, freestanding campfire using only a traditional bow-drill, flint-and-steel striking kit, or magnifying lens.
Presidents Note: You'll spend 95% of your time making smoke... and 5% trying not to lose the fire you finally earned.

Title: Continental Drift
Vibe: Each continent offers its own landscapes, languages, wildlife, traditions, and history. From bustling cities to remote wilderness, ancient ruins to towering mountains, every journey broadens your understanding of the planet and the people who call it home.
Why It Matters: Travel builds curiosity, adaptability, and appreciation for the incredible diversity of our planet. Experiencing different cultures firsthand encourages humility, expands worldviews, and creates memories that last a lifetime. Exploring multiple continents reinforces one of the WAC's greatest values: adventure isn't measured by distance alone, but by a willingness to step beyond the familiar and embrace the unknown.
Mission: Physically travel to, enter, and explore distinct geographical territories spanning at least six of Earth's seven continents.
Presidents Note: Jet lag is temporary. Bragging rights are forever.

Title: The Royale Trek
Vibe: With your entire camp on your back, each mile becomes an exercise in planning, perseverance, and self-reliance. The days are measured not by the clock, but by trail markers, ridgelines, and campsites. The reward isn't just reaching your destination—it's becoming part of one of the wildest landscapes in North America.
Why It Matters: Backpacking teaches independence in its purest form. Every meal, shelter, and piece of equipment must be thoughtfully selected, carried, and relied upon throughout the journey. Isle Royale adds another level of challenge by removing the convenience of roads, stores, and easy exits. Completing this adventure develops physical endurance, navigation, wilderness judgment, and confidence while creating an unforgettable appreciation for one of America's greatest wilderness treasures.
Mission: Plan, pack for, and successfully complete a continuous multi-day backpacking expedition covering a minimum of 10 trail miles within Isle Royale National Park while carrying all essential camping equipment and supplies.
Presidents Note: By Day Two, you'll stop asking how much farther it is... and start wondering why you ever packed that extra sweatshirt.

Title: Up Shits Creek – Without a Paddle
Vibe: Every bend reveals something new—a quiet stretch beneath towering trees, a swift riffle demanding your attention, a bald eagle overhead, or an unexpected logjam that reminds you nature always gets the final vote. Unlike a lake, where every stroke fights for progress, a river rewards rhythm, planning, and patience as the current becomes both your guide and your challenge.
Why It Matters: River travel develops endurance, navigation, hazard recognition, and sound decision-making while teaching respect for moving water. Planning a shuttle, reading current, avoiding strainers, and managing energy throughout a full day on the river all reinforce the importance of preparation before adventure. Completing a long-distance paddle also builds confidence, proving that steady effort—not speed—is often what carries you to the finish.
Mission: Plan, launch, and successfully complete a continuous single-day kayak or canoe expedition covering a minimum of 15 river miles on a recognized Michigan waterway.
Presidents Note: Anyone can paddle for thirty minutes. This badge starts around Hour Three.

Title: The Upper Hand
Vibe: The pace slows. The forests grow thicker. Lake Superior dominates the horizon, waterfalls spill through ancient rock, and small towns welcome travelers with pasties, stories, and unmistakable Yooper hospitality. Every stop reveals another reason why the Upper Peninsula feels less like another part of Michigan and more like its own rugged frontier.
Why It Matters: The Upper Peninsula represents some of the finest outdoor recreation and natural beauty in North America. Exploring multiple destinations encourages curiosity, appreciation for Michigan's rich history, and a deeper connection to its wilderness, mining heritage, and Great Lakes culture. More than a road trip, this adventure rewards planning, exploration, and the willingness to venture beyond familiar places in search of unforgettable experiences.
Mission: Cross the Mackinac Bridge and successfully complete a multi-stop Upper Peninsula expedition, visiting at least five major natural, historical, or cultural landmarks within Michigan's Upper Peninsula.
Presidents Note: You don't visit the Upper Peninsula... you disappear into it for a while

Title: Long Time, No See
Vibe: Long before the WAC existed, before your cabin stood in the woods, and before your own adventures were ever imagined, there were people whose choices shaped the life you live today. They worked, sacrificed, served, raised families, endured hardship, and built the foundation upon which future generations would stand.
Why It Matters: Understanding your family history builds appreciation for the generations whose perseverance, sacrifice, and decisions made your own life possible. Visiting ancestral graves transforms genealogy from names on a chart into real people with real stories. It encourages reflection, gratitude, and a deeper sense of identity while preserving family history that might otherwise be forgotten. Remembering those who came before us ensures their legacy continues with those who follow.
Mission: Locate, travel to, and physically document the gravesites of at least five direct-line ancestors buried within the State of Michigan, taking time to learn and record something about each person's life or legacy.
Presidents Note: One day you'll be someone else's family history. Live accordingly.

Title: Ancestry and Me
Vibe: This adventure invites you to leave the pages of a family tree behind and experience those places firsthand. Walk the streets they once walked. Visit the churches where they worshipped, the towns they called home, the ports where they arrived, or the archives where their names still live on.
Why It Matters: Family history is more than names and dates—it is the story of perseverance, sacrifice, and opportunity passed from one generation to the next. Visiting the places that shaped your ancestors creates a deeper appreciation for their lives and preserves family traditions that might otherwise disappear. This adventure strengthens your connection to your heritage while reminding future generations that understanding where we came from helps guide where we are going.
Mission: Plan and complete a dedicated heritage expedition to a city, county archive, ancestral homeland, or historically significant location outside Michigan that is directly connected to your family's immigration, settlement, or cultural history.
Presidents Note: Every family story started somewhere. Go find page one.

Title: Peak Performance
Vibe: Hidden deep within Baraga County, Mt. Arvon quietly claims the title of Michigan's highest point—not through dramatic peaks, but through rugged forests, winding logging roads, and peaceful wilderness that few people ever experience.
Why It Matters: High points have long inspired exploration, curiosity, and a sense of accomplishment. Reaching Mt. Arvon encourages route planning, backcountry navigation, and an appreciation for Michigan's often-overlooked wilderness. The journey reminds us that meaningful adventures don't always require extreme elevation—they simply require the willingness to go somewhere few others have.
Mission: Successfully navigate the backcountry roads and trails of Baraga County and physically hike to the official summit monument marking Mt. Arvon, the highest natural point in the State of Michigan.
Presidents Note: It may not be Everest, but it's ours.

Title: Say Cheese!
Vibe: They're tucked away along winding country roads, overlooking vineyards, hidden inside century-old barns, or crafted in small family businesses where recipes and traditions have been handed down for generations. Every stop tells a story—of local soil, changing seasons, hardworking families, and the pride that comes from making something by hand.
Why It Matters: Supporting local producers strengthens Michigan's agricultural communities and preserves traditions that make the state unique. Visiting independent wineries, creameries, and artisan food producers provides a firsthand appreciation for the craftsmanship, science, and dedication behind every bottle, wheel, or handcrafted product. More importantly, it reminds us that great adventures don't always require climbing mountains—sometimes they're found around a tasting table shared with family and friends.
Mission: Plan and execute a dedicated culinary road trip visiting at least three independent Michigan wineries, artisanal creameries, cideries, breweries, or other WAC Board-approved food producers.
Presidents Note: Life's too short for bad cheese and cheap wine.

Title: Ocean Commotion
Vibe: There’s something unforgettable about stepping into an ocean you've never experienced before. The temperature is different, the waves have their own personality, and the endless horizon reminds you just how big the world really is. This adventure isn't just about getting your feet wet—it's about collecting experiences from some of the most iconic waters on Earth.
Why It Matters: Every coastline tells a different story. The Atlantic, Pacific, and Gulf each have their own history, ecosystems, and character. By swimming in each one, you're marking your travels with an experience that's personal, memorable, and impossible to replicate from the shore. The world is meant to be explored, and sometimes the best way to experience it is to dive right in.
Mission: Successfully swim in the open waters of three distinct major bodies of water: the Atlantic Ocean, Pacific Ocean, and Gulf of America.
Presidents Note: Let's establish one important rule... walking ankle-deep through the surf while holding your flip-flops does not count.

Title: Pier Pressure
Vibe: As the shoreline fades into the distance, everyday responsibilities disappear over the horizon. New countries, unfamiliar cultures, incredible food, tropical beaches, and unforgettable sunsets all become part of the journey. But the real adventure isn't simply where the ship goes—it's who you're sharing the experience with.
Why It Matters: Travel strengthens friendships, broadens perspectives, and creates shared experiences that become family legends. Cruising offers the unique opportunity to experience multiple destinations while living, exploring, and relaxing together as a group. This adventure encourages members to step outside their daily routines, experience different cultures, and reinforce one of the WAC's core values: life's greatest adventures are even better when they're shared.
Mission: Embark on and completely execute a multi-day international cruise ship voyage alongside other WAC members.
Presidents Note: The hardest decision all week should be buffet... or buffet again.

Title: The Triple Crown
Vibe: The Triple Crown isn't earned through a single expedition but through a lifetime of curiosity, planning, and unforgettable journeys. It represents the explorer who has crossed oceans, experienced new cultures, stood on multiple continents, and shared incredible adventures with fellow WAC members along the way.
Why It Matters: The WAC was never intended to keep adventure within the property lines. It exists to inspire members to explore the world, chase ambitious goals, and return home with stories worth sharing. Completing these three signature adventures demonstrates perseverance, curiosity, and a commitment to living a life filled with meaningful experiences. It's more than earning three badges—it's proving you've embraced the spirit of adventure.
Mission: Successfully complete these three WAC adventures: The Original Eight, Continental Drift, Pier Pressure.
Presidents Note: Each of these adventures demands something different. One tests your planning, another your willingness to travel, and another your ability to step into experiences most people only talk about. Finish all three, and you've officially entered a very small group of WAC members who looked at the biggest adventures in the book and simply said, "Challenge accepted."

Title: The Freshwater Five
Vibe: The Great Lakes are more than just bodies of water—they're inland seas that have shaped the history, economy, and identity of Michigan for generations. This challenge invites you to experience each one firsthand, from the crystal-clear waters of Superior to the rolling waves of Erie, before finishing with one final test of courage... the legendary Polar Bear Dive.
Why It Matters: Michigan is unlike anywhere else in the world. Surrounded by four of the five Great Lakes and connected to the fifth, it offers a relationship with freshwater that few places can match. Visiting and swimming in each lake creates an appreciation for their unique beauty, power, and history, while the Polar Bear Dive serves as a reminder that some of life's greatest memories come from stepping well outside your comfort zone.
Mission: 1. Physically travel to and swim in all five Great Lakes (Superior, Michigan, Huron, Erie, and Ontario). 2. Complete a Polar Bear Dive.
Presidents Note: Let's be honest... Lake Superior isn't cold. It's a personality test.

Title: The Long Haul
Vibe: The objective isn't speed—it's precision. Every stop, every traffic delay, every construction zone, and every fuel break must be anticipated if you're going to arrive on time. Along the way, conversations flow over the radio, roadside landmarks blur into memories, and anticipation builds with every mile until the towers of the Mackinac Bridge finally appear on the horizon.
Why It Matters: Good planning consistently outperforms rushed execution. This challenge reinforces route planning, time management, teamwork, communication, and disciplined decision-making under changing conditions. It also demonstrates an important WAC principle: the goal isn't to arrive first—it's to arrive safely, together, and exactly when you intended.
Mission: Drive solo or as part of a WAC caravan from your home to the Mackinac Bridge toll plaza, arriving within the official target arrival window established by the WAC President.
Presidents Note: No battle plan survives the first construction zone

Title: Rapid Response
Vibe: Every rapid has a name, a personality, and a lesson waiting to be learned. Some demand precision. Others reward teamwork. All demand respect. Between the crashing waves and moments of quiet drift, you'll discover why whitewater paddlers return to rivers year after year—not because they're easy, but because they're unforgettable.
Why It Matters: Whitewater paddling develops teamwork, communication, confidence, and sound decision-making under pressure. Rivers are dynamic environments that require participants to assess hazards, respond to changing conditions, and trust both their equipment and their teammates. Whether navigating rapids with a guide or as part of an experienced crew, the challenge reinforces preparation, respect for nature, and the understanding that successful adventures depend on skill—not luck.
Mission: Successfully complete a guided or independently organized whitewater expedition, navigating a verified Class III or Class IV river using an approved raft or whitewater kayak while wearing all required safety equipment.
Presidents Note: It's called whitewater because brown-pants rapids didn't test well.

Title: Grease Monkey
Vibe: Every great adventure eventually leads to the same question: "Now what do we do?" Flat tires, dead batteries, and unexpected breakdowns don't care if you're headed to deer camp, the WAC, or a family vacation. This challenge is about becoming the person everyone hopes is in the vehicle when something goes wrong.
Why It Matters: Basic vehicle maintenance is one of the most practical life skills you can learn. It saves time, money, and frustration while giving you the confidence to handle common roadside problems safely. More importantly, it prepares you to help family, friends, or even a complete stranger when they need it most. Self-reliance has always been part of the WAC spirit, and this adventure puts that philosophy into practice.
Mission: Be able to perform if needed, the following automotive maintenance tasks: Jumpstart a vehicle, change a tire, Check and change the oil, locate the fuse box and change fuse, locate windshield washer fluid and refill, check air in tires, check and replace air filter.
Presidents Note: Let's be honest... somewhere out there is a person who thinks windshield washer fluid magically refills itself. Don't be that person.

Title: Shock Therapy
Vibe: A dark trail suddenly glows with light. A solar panel quietly charges batteries for the weekend. A remote hunting blind gains electricity where none existed before. Understanding how electricity works—and more importantly, how to use it safely—is one of the most practical modern skills an outdoorsman can possess.
Why It Matters: Electricity powers nearly every aspect of modern life, from cabins and workshops to off-grid camps and hunting properties. Learning to safely wire and troubleshoot simple systems builds confidence, encourages self-reliance, and develops practical problem-solving skills that can save both time and money. At the same time, understanding electricity also means respecting it. Even low-energy systems deserve careful planning and safe work habits because the best electrical projects are the ones that work reliably without anyone getting hurt.
Mission: Successfully wire, test, and safely place into service an electrical circuit, off-grid solar charging system, or multi-point lighting installation for use on WAC property. OR Touch an electric fence.
Presidents Note: If you choose the electric fence option... congratulations, you've officially tested the circuit.

Title: Short Circuit
Vibe: The WAC has never been afraid to try something new. Whether it's creating a scoreboard, designing a new game, building a family app, or finding a better way to organize adventures, innovation keeps the WAC growing. This challenge is about solving a problem, making life a little easier, or creating something that brings people together through technology.
Why It Matters: Technology isn't just for work—it's a powerful tool for preserving traditions, organizing events, and creating new experiences. Learning to build or improve software encourages creativity, problem-solving, and lifelong learning while leaving a lasting contribution that future WAC members can continue to enjoy. The best projects aren't always the most complicated; they're the ones that make a difference.
Mission: Come up with and implement a new software or computer program for use at the WAC.
Presidents Note: I'll let you in on a little secret... you don't have to be a computer genius to complete this adventure. You just have to look at something and say, "There has to be a better way."

Title: Can You Hear Me Know?
Vibe: Whether coordinating a hunt, checking in from a remote trail, communicating across the compound, or simply learning a timeless skill, radio remains one of the most dependable ways to stay connected when modern technology falls short. This challenge takes you beyond simply pressing a button—it teaches you how to build, configure, and operate a communications system that can serve the WAC for years to come.
Why It Matters: Reliable communication is one of the cornerstones of safety, preparedness, and teamwork. Learning how to assemble and operate a radio system develops technical problem-solving skills while reinforcing the importance of planning and redundancy when working or recreating outdoors. Whether used for emergency preparedness, property coordination, or simply expanding your knowledge, radio communication remains a valuable skill that continues to connect people when other systems cannot.
Mission: Successfully assemble and configure a functional base station or mobile radio system and establish clear two-way voice communications with another station.
Presidents Note: If your first transmission is, 'Can anybody hear me?'... congratulations, you're officially a radio operator.

Title: Train of Thought
Vibe: Adventure doesn't always require a passport. Sometimes all it takes is unfolding a new map, claiming a few railway routes, and setting off across a different corner of the world. Every version of Ticket to Ride offers a new landscape, new strategies, and new stories waiting to be told. The destination may change, but the laughter, friendly competition, and inevitable cries of "You stole my route!" remain the same.
Why It Matters: Games have a unique way of bringing people together. Ticket to Ride encourages planning, problem-solving, and a little friendly rivalry while introducing players to different countries and regions of the world. More importantly, it creates an opportunity to disconnect from screens, gather around the table, and enjoy each other's company—something that's always been at the heart of the WAC.
Mission: Play three different versions (countries) of the board game Ticket to Ride.
Presidents Note: If you've never played Ticket to Ride, let me give you a little warning... this game has ended more friendships than Monopoly ever dreamed of.

Title: Breaker Breaker
Vibe: Long before smartphones and GPS apps, road trips, hunting camps, and off-road expeditions stayed connected through the crackle of a radio microphone. Every call sign, every location update, and every "copy that" helped keep the group moving as one.
Why It Matters: Effective radio communication builds teamwork, situational awareness, and leadership. Learning proper radio etiquette, concise transmissions, and organized convoy procedures prepares members for everything from recreational trail rides to emergency situations where cell service may not exist. More importantly, it reinforces one of the WAC's core principles: clear communication keeps everyone safe, informed, and working toward the same objective.
Mission: Coordinate and execute a multi-vehicle WAC convoy or compound operation using CB or GMRS radios. Participants must use assigned radio handles, provide clear location and status updates, and demonstrate proper radio etiquette and channel changes throughout the operation.
Presidents Note: Your radio handle cannot be 'Big Daddy' unless everyone else votes for it.
